racecards.race_name: Prix Robert Lejeune (Hurdle) (Grade 3) (3yo Colts & Geldings) (Turf)

runners.breeder: Mme A Kepa & Mlle K A Kepa

runners.owner: Mme Dominique Bureller & Mme Brigitte Lamazou-Laresse
Philippe Georges Dumas, Y Fouin & Frederic Duvivier
Simon Munir, Haras De Saint-Voir & Isaac Souede

racecards.race_name: Almins Vantage/Lallemand Magniva Classic EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xxiii & M Hulin
runners.comment: Respected newcomer from a leading yard enjoying a good year with 2yos
runners.spotlight: 48,000gns half-brother to 7f winner Bill Silvers (RPR 99); respected newcomer from a leading yard enjoying a good year with 2yos.

runners.comment: Half-brother to four winners; the betting should guide regarding expectations
runners.spotlight: 5,000euros yearling; half-brother to winners Coco Bear (6f including 2yo; RPR 85), Three Yorkshiremen (1m-1m3f including AW; 79), Si Senior (5.7f 2yo/9.7f; 72) and Primeiro Boy (5f turf 2yo/7f AW; 68); the betting should be a good guide as to expectations.

runners.comment: Promising second on Hamilton debut last month and sets good standard for rest to aim at
runners.spotlight: Frankel colt who made a pleasing start to his career when going down by only a neck over the extended 1m at Hamilton (good to firm) 27 days ago; will set a good standard for the rest if making the anticipated improvement.

runners.comment: The stable hasn't had a first-time-out 2yo winner in 2025 so a watching brief is the call
runners.spotlight: 50,000euros purchase; second foal; half-brother to Italian 6f-7.5f winner (including 2yo) Cape Alava; dam placed 1m/1m2f France (RPR 72), half-sister to smart 1m4f-2m1f Flat and useful 2m-2m4f hurdle winner Burning Victory; the stable hasn't had a first-time-out 2yo winner in 2025.

runners.comment: Fair form both starts but more needed to trouble Mr Colonel
runners.spotlight: Showed ability, despite pulling hard, when third in a small field at Beverley (7.5f, good to firm) on debut; beaten 8l but stepped up on that form when seventh of 13 in the valuable Convivial maiden at the York Ebor meeting (7f, good to firm) last month; more needed to seriously trouble Mr Colonel, though.

quotes.race: Almins Vantage/Lallemand Magniva Classic EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: We've thought a bit about Sea The Power for a long time. The plan was always to make the running. York just probably didn't suit him on the day, he was just run off his feet a little bit early on and he'll go a mile and a quarter or a mile and a half no problem - Adrian Keatley, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He has a big future. Things didn't go well for him at Beverley last week. They crawled down the hill and he pulled hard. He'll come on a ton for that introduction and I'm now looking at the Convivial Maiden at York. He needs 7f or a mile at this stage. 07-08-25

runners.comment: Well bred but remote fifth on 7f debut in July; subsequently gelded
runners.spotlight: Has some smart winners in his pedigree but he weakened to finish a remote fifth of six on 7f Musselburgh debut (good; 9-1) in July; subsequently gelded; the yard won this last year but could still only consider if the betting suggests that a lot better is expected this time.

runners.comment: Would be a surprise winner on debut
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to connections' 5f winner Royal Duke (RPR 64); dam also a 7f winner (77) in these silks; would be a surprise winner on debut.

runners.owner: T Cottier, On Top Racing, Amity Fin
runners.comment: Worth a betting check for yard that tasted success in this in 2023
runners.spotlight: By Ulysses and the fourth foal of a 6f/7f AW winner (including 2yo; RPR 90); the yard won this race in 2023 so she's worth a check in the betting.

racecards.verdict: If MR COLONEL builds on his promising debut second, as seems likely, he should prove hard to beat.
racecards.betting_forecast: 1/2 Mr Colonel, 7/1 Gaelic Approach, 8/1 Sea The Power, 10/1 Northern Brave, 16/1 King Of Roses,  Unchartedterritory, 22/1 Yorkies Dream, 50/1 Royal Baroness

racecards.race_name: Jackie And Janet Mack 65th Anniversary Handicap

runners.comment: Well handicapped, in form and drops in class; rain would suit; plenty to recommend him
runners.spotlight: Beat 16 rivals when winning a soft-ground Class 3 handicap at York last October and he is now 7lb lower; good recent efforts at Chepstow and Haydock and this represents a drop in class; rain would be a further boost to his chances and he is high on the list.

runners.comment: Better on AW but there has been some promise on turf this year; rain perhaps not ideal
runners.spotlight: Better on AW than turf but that is reflected in his mark and there have been some promising signs on the grass this summer, including a close fifth at Haydock 13 days ago (5f, good); has a bit more to prove on slow ground (one fair run) but rider takes off a valuable 5lb and he's capable.

quotes.race: £30 In Free Bets With BetUK Handicap
quotes.quote: They weren't the tactics we really wanted to employ but Monks Dream did the same thing the last day, he just rushed the gates before they opened. In a race like that over five furlongs when you miss the beat you're going to be held up. The way the race worked out, perhaps if he was handy he might not have finished as well as he did. Now let's see if he can transfer that form to grass - Sean Kirrane, jockey, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

stable_tour.quote: He won't run early as he's been very backward. It was the same last year, but he progressed well when he came out and is a tough horse. He's one for six-furlong races in mid-season. 23-04-24

runners.comment: Promise in three AW runs (6f) this summer; could have more to offer over 5f on turf
runners.spotlight: Similar form in three 6f AW runs in May/June; unexposed and should be quick enough for 5f; brings a different profile to her rivals and market confidence would look significant.

runners.breeder: Boyce Bloodstock & Mrs C E Percival
runners.comment: C&D win on stable debut last month; still feasibly treated and soft ground won't hurt
runners.spotlight: Never been short of ability and he has been rated higher in the past; perfect start for this yard when beating Royal Duke over C&D last month; 5lb rise not insurmountable and rain won't be an issue; contender.

quotes.race: Amazing Sri Lanka Handicap (A Jockey Club Grassroots Series Sprint Qualifier)
quotes.quote: I'd love to say this was the plan, but it was more to have a run and then bring him back to the race he won last year. I knew he was pretty sharp, on Monday he dropped our lad who rides him every day and galloped round the field like a good'un, which gave him that last piece of work in a way. I said to his owner the morning, 'Don't think this horse hasn't got a chance', because he was as fit as I could get him at this stage. Sure enough, he's shown that. Harb is wonderful, I love him to bits, but I've also got another horse I'm really excited about called Harry Brown. I've got really high hopes for later in the year - Peter Crate, trainer.

quotes.race: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Handicap
quotes.quote: Harb has had a bit of a break as we thought he needed it and Peter said about running here so I said 'go on' and he has done it well. When Tom (Queally, jockey) got off him at Epsom he said we should try a mile - there was no way on God's earth he was going to get it, but you have to listen to the likes of Tom and give the horse a chance.I actually walked away as everywhere he was going he was getting blocked in, but he has ended up doing that well - Pat Gallagher, owner.

stable_tour.quote: Has been working well and will run soon, 18-04-21

runners.comment: Capable off this mark and big field could help him settle; rain would help; not ruled out
runners.spotlight: Soft-ground winner for the Quinns as a 2yo; off the mark for this yard when leading late on at Musselburgh in July (5f, good) and he's only 1lb higher today; can pull hard but the big field could help in that regard; won't mind rain and he's not ruled out.

runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
runners.comment: No win since debut but latest effort more encouraging and rain won't be an issue
runners.spotlight: Sole win came on debut at Carlisle (5f, good to firm) in June 2023; ran his best race for some time when fourth at Chester last week (6f, good; cheekpieces first time) and rain won't hurt; not without hope.

runners.comment: More promising signs 12 days ago; blinkers now added; dangerous mark; rain a big plus
runners.spotlight: Only midfield in the Ayr Silver Cup 12 months ago but he was rated 14lb higher at the time; more promising signs at Wolverhampton 12 days ago (tongue tied first time) and the prospect of rain is a big positive; Tom Marquand booked and blinkers given a go; interesting.

quotes.race: cavani.co.uk The Dapper Dash For Cash Handicap
quotes.quote: Alex gave Never Dark a really good ride. He had a feather weight and not many jockeys can do that weight so we were looking for a good apprentice who can claim. And he gave him a lovely, straightforward ride. We had him in at Ripon as well but it's undulating there and it doesn't suit every horse. The key to this horse is he handles dig in the ground and he's come to himself this year. He seems to have found a bit of confidence - Iain Jardine, trainer.

quotes.race: Unibet Thanks The Frontline Workers Handicap
quotes.quote: Never Dark jumped really well and never saw another rival after that. He's pretty sensible within himself and could go back over six but I'd like to see him running over five furlongs on the grass. The whole family all progressed with age and over the winter he has strengthened, and I would imagine he'll take a big step forward - David Probert, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: These older sprinters can always bounce back and, while we might not have seen the best of him this time, he hasn't had his ground. He wants a bit of juice or the all-weather and he's well handicapped. He probably wants some headgear on now. 10-09-25

runners.comment: 6f AW winner; sharper for return to action last month; best turf form on faster ground
runners.spotlight: 6f AW win last November came off just 1lb lower; looked as though the run would bring her on when fourth of five at Hamilton four weeks ago (6f, good to firm); she will need an end to end gallop back down to 5f but it seems likely that she has a bigger effort in her at some point soon; more to prove on ground slower than good.

quotes.race: Jackie And Janet Mack 65th Anniversary Handicap
quotes.quote: I told Lauren [Young, jockey] to get cover and play Dandy Magic late. She's probably better at six furlongs and needed every yard of the five but she got there - Jim Goldie, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She won for us on the all-weather but I think she's paid for it. When you run a young horse on the all-weather they can feel it after a few runs. We've givenher a little break and we'll probably bring her back for a backend campaign. We probably ran her once or twice too often on the Tapeta. I think she's quite talented and if we can get her 100 per cent sound then she'll progress again. 07-05-25

runners.owner: Making Headway Racing, Cassie And Wilson

prev_owners.owner: Making Headway Racing And K Scott

runners.comment: Return to well-run 5f will suit; only 1 win from 20 starts so more of a place proposition
runners.spotlight: Split Royal Duke and Happier when second at Musselburgh last month (5f, good) and he didn't enjoy a clean run through on that occasion; not so good over 6f last time and 2lb higher mark has now kicked in; strongly run 5f should suit but overall record suggests he's more of a place proposition.

stable_tour.quote: We got him from the online sales in February. We know the jockey PJ McDonald quite well and he's ridden him previously. He was bad debt in his last yard which is why he was for sale. It's taken us a few runs to get to know him. In his last run, at Ayr last week, he was fifth but it was a very hot race and I really think he's ready to kick on and win in his next few runs. 17-07-25

runners.owner: John Kelly, John McNeill & Kevin Mowatt
runners.comment: Ten-race maiden but he has run well in 4 of 5 starts this year; slow ground a slight query
runners.spotlight: Still seeking his first win after ten attempts but he has run to a fair level in four of his five starts this season; suitability of ground slower than good a slight query.

runners.comment: Promise in Hamilton maidens as a 2yo; absent 432 days; tough task on handicap debut
runners.spotlight: Finished in the money in two 5f Hamilton maidens last summer; makes his handicap debut after a 432-day absence (been gelded) and it will be a fine performance to win a race such as this after the layoff.

runners.comment: Best form this year on AW but on a lowly mark and had few chances on slower than good
runners.spotlight: Won twice on turf as a 2yo but his win this spring came on AW; perhaps sharper for his recent fourth at Newcastle and he has had few chances on slower than good on turf; due to be 2lb lower in the future but still not discounted given his raw ability.

runners.comment: Comfortable win at Catterick last month and two solid 3rds have followed; more needed here
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer; ready win in a small field at Catterick last month (5f, good) and she has backed that up with two close thirds; behind Royal Duke last time and although she's better off at the weights, she will need to raise her game to take this.

runners.comment: Comes here on the up but this is a deeper race and he may prove vulnerable
runners.spotlight: C&D win in July and then defied an 11lb higher mark at Musselburgh three weeks ago; still improving but he is in deeper today.

runners.breeder: Mr & Mrs P & J Hopper, Mrs M Morris
runners.comment: Three wins this year, two of them here; handicapper may just have her measure now
runners.spotlight: Three wins this summer, two of them at this track (both 6f, good); fully effective at 5f and on good to soft ground but the handicapper may just have her measure for now.

runners.owner: S Bulmer, P Hebdon & R Taylor
runners.comment: 0-11; best efforts this year give him a squeak but quiet in recent starts; new headgear
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden; good efforts at Redcar and Ripon in midsummer (6f; first two runs in cheekpieces) but it has been a struggle more recently; change of headgear may help but Tim Easterby saddles more obvious contenders.

runners.owner: William Burns & Miss L A Perratt
runners.comment: Multiple AW winner but 0-36 on turf and opposable in this field
runners.spotlight: Multiple AW winner but 0-36 on turf and this looks a tough race in which to try and right that wrong.

runners.breeder: D D & Mrs J P Clee
runners.comment: Inconsistent in 2025 and ran poorly at the weekend; tough race to bounce back in
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent in 2025 and he stopped quickly at Musselburgh on Saturday; this looks a tough race in which to rebound.

racecards.verdict: Never Dark and VINCE L'AMOUR are well treated on last autumn's slow-ground form and look the pair to concentrate on.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/2 Vince L'Amour, 7/1 Harb, 8/1 Jm Jhingree,  Never Dark, 10/1 Monks Dream,  Your Love,  Emperor's Son, 12/1 Lucius Aurelius, 14/1 Ganesha,  Happier,  Royal Duke,  Thunderstorm Katie, 16/1 Orbital Chime,  Dandy Magic, 20/1 Joshuas Dream, 25/1 Spring Corn, 33/1 Pockley,  Sixcor

racecards.race_name: Ladbrokes "Free Bet At Ayr" Kilkerran Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

prev_owners.owner: Exors Of The Late Mary, Lady Manton

runners.comment: Needs to get back on track but he's been contesting hot races and is not written off
runners.spotlight: Won at Ripon (1m2f, good) in April and continued in good form in May; also effective on soft ground; down the field on both runs this summer and needs to get back on track, but those were hotter races than today's and he's not written off following a 68-day break.

quotes.race: Coral Kincsem Handicap
quotes.quote: Have Secret has run a cracking race, which he has done all season. He's a horse who is getting better with racing, but he keeps just finding one or two. But we are delighted with him - Richard Fahey, trainer.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs EBF Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: Have Secret tends to race behind the bridle but keeps finding. The step up in trip helped him and he handled the ground. Oisin Orr, jockey.

quotes.race: Sky Bet Sunday Series Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: Have Secret deserved that after he ran well at Chester last week on a course that didn't suit him. He was going to go up the ratings so we thought we'd come here and he's going to go up even further now. He'll make a nice 3yo next year - Robin O'Ryan, assistant to trainer Richard Fahey.

runners.comment: 2-2 since upped to about this trip, winning in a big field at York most recently
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won when upped to 1m2f at York (good) in July and followed up in a big field there (1m2f, good to firm) at the Ebor meeting; has also won here and shown promise on soft ground; unexposed at today's trip and has gone up just 3lb for last time; can make another bold bid.

runners.comment: 2-2 when encountering slow ground in handicaps (both last autumn); worth a second look
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer whose form has been mixed since he won at Redcar (1m2f, good to firm) in May; however, he's 2-2 when encountering slow ground in handicaps (both wins last autumn) so is well worth a second look in view of today's conditions; wears a first-time tongue-tie.

quotes.race: Racing TV Zetland Gold Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
quotes.quote: Liberty Coach is a very likeable horse and I'm delighted the owners kept him, as there were a lot of people trying to buy him last year. It's horses like him we're trying to hold on to. He had a very good year last year and found his mojo at the back end. We felt he'd come on from last time, and he loves galloping tracks like this. He'll want three weeks before we go again. We'll be pitching him in to run in races for better money now, which is great - John Quinn, joint-trainer.

quotes.race: Believe Specialist Finance Apprentice Handicap
quotes.quote: It's nice to get Liberty Coach's head in front. He's showed good ability in maidens and handicaps and it was frustrating he had yet to be a winner before now. He's quite a big horse and I'd be hopeful he could keep progressing - Sean Quinn, joint-trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's a big unraced gelding who's likely to start off over 1m2f at Doncaster this weekend. I quite like him and he has the make and shape of a nice middle-distance horse. 20-03-24

runners.comment: No surprise if this lightly raced 3yo turns things around sooner rather than later
runners.spotlight: 2-4 in last year's 2yo campaign (including a win on good to soft) and he reappeared with a win at Chester (7.5f, good) in May; down the field on both starts since (1m2f/7.5f, good to firm) but it will be no surprise if this lightly raced 3yo turns things around sooner rather than later.

quotes.race: Ladbrokes "Free Bet At Ayr" Kilkerran Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
quotes.quote: We've always liked El Burhan and I'm very grateful to his owners for being so patient. He was a winner at Chester at the May meeting then he just wasn't quite right at Ascot, We had to give him a bit of time off after that and we thought we'd go back to Chester, where it worked, but he got drawn in the car park. He's really seen the trip out well and that was the key to him today. A bit of give in the ground is also key and it's a nice time of year to have a horse who handles ease in the ground. He's in the Cambridgeshire - that's probably a little quick for him but there are similar ten-furlong handicaps and he's one to take into next year if his owner would like to. He's from a late-maturing family and he's coming good at the back-end of his three-year-old career. It's not beyond the realms of possibility that he could be better than a handicapper, he's going the right way for sure and I think the better the race the better he'll be - George Boughey, trainer.El Burhan won well today. I think he appreciated a bit more of a galloping track. He's a big boy and last time at Chester, albeit he won there at the start of the year, it looked like it was a bit sharp and it was a lesser trip. It all happened a little bit fast for him. Ideally I'd have liked to have been a spot further forward today but he races a little bit lazily and I didn't want to go firing him up there up in trip so I ended up midway. The pace was strong, he travelled round well, I hit the front and he idled but he kept going - Billy Loughnane, jockey.

quotes.race: Louise Keegan Handicap
quotes.quote: El Burhan got the split just in time and did well. He's a big horse and we feel he should appreciate going up in trip. It was a good pot and a good bit of placing. We've not got to the bottom of him just yet - Jim Crowley, rider.

quotes.race: Matthew Ireland Memorial EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: El Burhan took a bit of time to get into his stride but won well. Jim has always thought he'll stay further next year and he does look like a horse for the future. I'll speak to [owner] Sheikha Hissa and George and we'll find something for him, and I'd say he wants a mile now. Hopefully, he's very much a horse to look forward to as a three-year-old. He's very much going the right way; we'll see if he's up to Listed class or better next. We'll make sure to mind him this season - Angus Gold, owner's racing manager.

stable_tour.quote: He was a good winner at Chester over a sharp seven and a half furlongs and has done most of his running around Beverley, Chester and Goodwood. I think we'll see him in a better light on a big, galloping track with a strong pace to aim at.  09-06-25

runners.comment: Getting back on slow ground is a positive but a shorter trip may be preferable
runners.spotlight: Getting back on slow ground is a positive and she's 5lb lower than when winning at Haydock (1m, soft) last September, but she hasn't been at the top of her game in a light campaign and a shorter trip may be preferable.

stable_tour.quote: She's going to be campaigned in the autumn because she wants soft ground, so there should be plenty of races for her. We got a nice bit of black type with her last year and there are options for her back in France at the end of the year. We're just trying to work on her mark and we need to get her out on the ground to really find out. If she gets into a bit of form, she could go for something like the Cambridgeshire. 07-08-25

runners.comment: Close third at York recently but the return to slow ground isn't an obvious positive
runners.spotlight: His last six outings have all been at York, where he was a close third 11 days ago (1m2f, good) off this reduced mark; he has some good form here but the return to slow ground isn't an obvious positive.

quotes.race: Sky Bet Club Handicap
quotes.quote: Dark Moon Rising is a talented horse when he's on a good day. He was very well handicapped on his old form. I don't know what was up with him, he hasn't been performing. But he travelled really strongly and galloped all the way to the line. He won cosily - Shane Gray, rider, talking to ITV.

quotes.race: Pure Broadband EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Dark Moon Rising is a work in progress, he was very green first time. I thought he'd be a bit wiser today but he was green again. But once he passed one he picked the bridle up and he travelled well into the race. He came good from two down, he knuckled down and galloped through the line well. He'll keep improving with racing. He's a big, laid back horse at home, he's just going to have to learn with racing - Kevin Ryan, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He had some high-class form at three and if he can revert to the form he showed in the Dante he's on a very fair mark and could be a nice horse for handicaps. I think ten furlongs is his best trip and he'll benefit from getting back on a conventional track after not handling Pontefract last time. We retain a lot of faith in him. 03-05-23

runners.owner: S Bulmer, P Hebdon & R Taylor
runners.comment: Won this last year and runner-up on three of his nine races this term; could be in the mix
runners.spotlight: 0-9 this season and inconsistent but runner-up three times in 2025 and he won this last year (good); effective on good to soft and possibly okay on soft; in the mix if he's on a going day in first-time cheekpieces.

stable_tour.quote: He's a nice colt by Cracksman who's had only one run, finishing fifth at York. We like him and you'd think he'll be a middle-distance horse. 23-04-24

runners.comment: Clear 2nd to well-handicapped winner over C&D in July; could play leading role back here
runners.spotlight: Won 2yo debut here (1m, good) last September and clear second behind well-handicapped winner in handicap over this C&D (good) in July; has shown promise on good to soft (unraced on softer); close fifth at York 11 days ago and he could play a leading role now back here.

quotes.race: Western House Hotel EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Hornsea Bay looked impressive. His home work had been pretty good. If you debut them over a mile at this time of year you like to think they'll stay ten furlongs and maybe a mile and a half. We might run him in another novice for experience. He could be nice next year, he's got a good pedigree as well - David O'Meara, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's very exciting - so much so I've entered him in the Dante. He was very good last year when he won and then he wasn't beaten far giving away a penalty. He got a bit hot before his comeback run at Nottingham on Saturday in a strong race. He ran fine and was maybe a bit fresh for his first run. We'll look at a nice mile handicap and I'm sure he can step up in trip as the season goes on. 14-04-25

We bought him at the Arqana Breeze-Up Sale in May. He has a really classy Cheveley Park pedigree by a quality stallion out of a Listed-class mare. He's a good-looking horse with lots of size and scope and we're really excited by him. He'd been working nicely going into his debut at Ayr last week but he delivered more than expected by winning easily. We'll probably look to give him one more run from mid-October and there are a few options for him, including the Silver Tankard at Pontefract. He's bred to be a three-year-old over middle distances and gives every indication that's exactly what he'll be. He shows a lot of promise and he's one to really look forward to next year. 26-09-24

runners.comment: 3yo who won in a first-time visor at Haydock in April but he hasn't kicked on from there
runners.spotlight: 3yo who won in a first-time visor at Haydock (1m, good) in April but he hasn't kicked on from there; needs to raise his game now tackling 1m2f for the first time; the visor replaces the blinkers adopted last time.

runners.comment: Prolific 4yo; close second at Doncaster last Thursday and runs before 5lb rise kicks in
runners.spotlight: Highly progressive 4yo who made it seven wins from his last nine starts when scoring at Southwell (1m3f, AW) last month, and he was a close second of 14 off this mark at Doncaster last Thursday (1m4f, good to soft; unraced on softer); reappears before 5lb rise kicks in and holds very solid claims.

runners.breeder: Epona Bloodstock Ltd And P A Byrne
runners.comment: Has dropped down the weights but he's failed to threaten on his last three outings
runners.spotlight: Close fourth in this race two years ago and he's well treated on form as recent as this July; however, he's failed to threaten on his last three outings and needs to turn things around.

quotes.race: Racehorse Lotto Handicap
quotes.quote: We've always thought a lot of Austrian Theory and he was a very talented two-year-old. I thought he was a real Golden Mile type for Goodwood last year but he can be a bit in and out. His run at Chester last weekend was a sign he was coming back to the boil and Joe Fanning was sublime on the front end. The straight track handicaps don't really suit him and, albeit the plan didn't come off last year, we'll be working back from the Golden Mile again - Charlie Johnston, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's a grand horse who we bought from the Horses In Training Sale in Newmarket. He's not quite ready to run yet, but we're looking forward to him this summer. I'm still learning about him, but I'd expect him to want a mile and good ground and to be running at some of the bigger meetings. 23-04-24

runners.comment: Well treated on this year's best AW form but has struggled on his last four outings
runners.spotlight: Four wins over 1m2f at Newcastle (AW) early this year and close fourth of 19 there (2m) in June when 3lb higher than today; however, he's struggled for form since (turf/Tapeta) and a major turnaround is required.

quotes.race: Gamble Responsibly At BetUK Handicap
quotes.quote: Midnight Lion loves it round here and is thriving at the minute. He's very straightforward - he wings the gates, he doesn't pull in front and when you get stuck into him he really keeps finding - Paul Mulrennan, jockey, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

quotes.race: Extra Winnings With BetUK Acca Club Handicap (Div I)
quotes.quote: We were hoping Midnight Lion would run like that as Paul [Mulrennan, jockey] had won on him over the course and distance. He developed a sinus problem and he's been on antibiotics so we were a bit negative about his chances. Fortunately he won, but he probably needs a tooth out, so it was quite impressive - Jim Goldie, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's been terrific. I had a chat with Mark Johnston about him and I pointed out to him that we've actually only got him back to where we had him, rated in the 80s and winning at Goodwood. He's a big galloper and possibly better at 1m2f than the 2m trip he ran over last time. The problem is he likes to dominate and then people can take you on and get you beat. If they leave him alone he's hard to pass and if you take him on you'll get beaten yourself, so it's a Catch 22. He seems to love the Tapeta surface, so we might head towards the Northumberland Plate or Vase. There's races for him this Saturday. We're bringing him back from a quiet spell but he's done brilliantly. 07-05-25

runners.comment: First run of the year but this 3yo's pedigree hints he could be better than opening mark
runners.spotlight: In the first three late last year in his three qualifying runs (1m/1m2f, AW/soft); it's a big ask to win a race this competitive on his first outing of the year but his trainer is very much among the winners and this 3yo's pedigree provides optimism he can be better than his opening mark.

racecards.verdict: It is hard to oppose the prolific CROWD QUAKE (nap), who came clear with an unexposed 3yo when suffering a rare defeat at Doncaster.
racecards.betting_forecast: 100/30 Crowd Quake, 5/1 Hornsea Bay, 11/2 Dain Ma Nut In, 6/1 El Burhan, 8/1 Financer, 10/1 Dark Moon Rising, 12/1 Have Secret,  Liberty Coach, 14/1 Cloth Of Gold, 16/1 Flying Finn, 25/1 Sex On Fire,  Austrian Theory, 66/1 Midnight Lion

racecards.race_name: British Stallion Studs Scottish Premier Series EBF Fillies' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen
runners.comment: Well held in Listed grade last time but may still have more to offer; respected
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who was soundly beaten in 1m4f Listed event at the York Ebor festival most recently; made all at Newmarket (1m2f, good to soft) earlier in August and may still have more to offer; respected back down in trip/class.

runners.owner: HRH Prince Faisal Bin Khaled & Najd Stud
runners.comment: Latest effort took her record to 3212; may do better still; remains of interest
runners.spotlight: Couldn't quite manage to justify favouritism in Lingfield handicap (1m1f, good to firm) last time but was far from discredited, taking form figures to 3212; steps up further in distance and may do better still; remains of interest.

quotes.race: JCT600 Kia Castleford Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Charming Princess got a comfortable lead at a tempo I wanted to dictate. I probably got a bit lonely the last half furlong but she stuck it out. She's a grand filly who tries very hard. She'll get a mile and a quarter and a bit more juice in the ground will probably suit. This track probably didn't help - it's very undulating and she's very leggy - so a flatter track over a mile and a quarter could potentially see more to come - Rossa Ryan, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.comment: Ran well over 1m on AW last time; something to prove in this scenario
runners.spotlight: Gained Irish success (7f) on AW and ran well in that sphere at Southwell (1m; second start for new yard) most recently; a possible danger if proving as effective back on turf and upped to 1m2f but that's the imponderable.

runners.comment: Gained her novice success in Scotland; better form is possibly still to come
runners.spotlight: Stablemate of Donna Nook; unraced on ground softer than good; ran respectably at Leicester (1m2f) last week, taking record to 32163; gained her novice success in Scotland and, with better form possibly still to come, enters calculations.

quotes.race: British Stallion Studs Scottish Premier Series EBF Fillies' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
quotes.quote: Brielle enjoyed the ground and she stayed well. I think she could even stay further. We could go back up to a mile and a half - David O'Meara, trainer.

quotes.race: Racing TV Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: I ran Brielle over a mile on debut, then a mile and a half, and dropped her back to this extended mile today. I was pleased with how she coped with the trip and how well she solved the race - David O'Meara, trainer.

runners.comment: May raise her game now handicapping for new yard; check the betting
runners.spotlight: Raced three times for William Haggas, failing to progress from Goodwood debut win (1m, good); may raise her game now handicapping for new yard and market support should be heeded; out of a 1m2f winner.

runners.comment: Won at Ayr in July but may be out of her depth in this grade
runners.spotlight: Record is 2-18; has shown all of her handicap form in lower grades; won at Ayr (1m5f, good) in July and is versatile as regards trip but may be out of her depth at this level.

runners.comment: Recent Musselburgh winner but well held here on Thursday and prefers drier ground
runners.spotlight: Beat four rivals with some ease at Musselburgh last month (1m4f, good) but was well beaten here on Thursday and drier ground suits her better.

stable_tour.quote: She's a progressive filly who has won over 1m2f and 1m4f this season and is still on the upgrade. She's out of a Galileo mare from a very good family, of which the granny was the best. She's up to a mark of 71 and I expect more progress. 12-09-22

racecards.verdict: Back down in trip/class, AEOLIAN is particularly interesting. Second choice is Brielle.
racecards.betting_forecast: Evs Aeolian, 100/30 Brielle, 9/2 Donna Nook, 8/1 Tsuki, 14/1 Sophiesticate, 16/1 Naturalia

racecards.race_name: RBC Brewin Dolphin Wealth Management Handicap (Div I)

runners.comment: On last winning mark but yet to return to peak form since joining this trainer
runners.spotlight: Won for the Crisfords off this mark at Chelmsford (1m2f, AW) in April but tailed off on final outing for that stable and has finished merely eighth in his two runs for this trainer; needs to raise his game.

runners.comment: Showed some spark in July but has a regressive profile and losing sequence is up to 15
runners.spotlight: He showed some spark when second at Newmarket (7f, good to soft) in July but hasn't shone since and has a regressive profile; losing sequence is now up to 15 and he needs to turn things around.

quotes.race: Racing Welfare Supporting Mental Health Awareness Restricted Maiden Stakes (For horses In Band D)
quotes.quote: We've worked back from Royal Ascot with Waiting All Night and he could run again before then. He's not just a five-furlong horse and we have a few more of Rajasinghe's progeny and other two-year-olds to run in the next few weeks, so we'll hopefully have a strong team for the meeting. It's hard to win from such a wide draw here, particularly on debut, so he's done very well and it shows the ability he has and attitude to go with it - Richard Spencer, trainer.

runners.comment: Good runs have been in the minority this year but he likes it here and could be involved
runners.spotlight: Good runs have been in the minority this year and he was soundly beaten at Doncaster last month, but seven days earlier he was second here (1m2f, good) and he won twice here in May 2024 (1m2f, good/good to firm); has been versatile ground-wise in the past; could be involved now back here.

stable_tour.quote: He went up 4lb to 74 after his last win at Ayr in May but then he's finished second in his last four races and has gone up another 7lb to 81. I'm thinking we need to change something and maybe try him over a bit further - he's been running over 1m2f - as he clearly looks to be in the handicapper's grip over that trip. 28-08-24

runners.comment: Disappointing on handicap debut at Haydock but promise previously and retains potential
runners.spotlight: Disappointing on handicap debut at Haydock (1m, good) last month but this 3yo was previously a promising third at Carlisle (1m, good to soft) and retains potential; no surprise if he bounces back with a big run.

runners.comment: 10yo who arrives in good heart but the weights rise and slow ground might not help
runners.spotlight: Easily won handicap for novice amateurs at Carlisle (1m, good) on September 1 before second of 13 under penalty at Newcastle (1m, AW); however, this 10yo is a further 5lb higher today and he may not want slow ground.

quotes.race: AJA Novice Flat Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (Jockeys With Three 3 Wins Or Fewer At Start Of Season)
quotes.quote: I was a bit unlucky when I rode Jewel Maker on Friday, but I got it right this time. I got held up and couldn't find the gaps, so we never got a proper go then. He travelled a lot better and I was more aware of how to get out of positions, so when I saw a gap I went for it. He did it easily - Rebecca Burley, rider, speaking to Racing TV.

quotes.race: Sky Bet Sunday Series Middle Distance Handicap
quotes.quote: This was a stronger race than Jewel Maker won at Carlisle and he picked them up nicely. He's a horse who needs cover early on as he can be a bit keen  - Sean Kirrane, jockey.

quotes.race: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Maiden
quotes.quote: Jewel Maker will appreciate much better ground and he's a colt we would expect to get black type with in the coming weeks over 7f to a mile. He's a good-actioned horse and he will come on considerably - Dermot Weld, trainer.

prev_owners.owner: The Childe Of Hale The Good Racing Co

runners.comment: 3yo who hit the front in final furlong over 7f at Doncaster; optimism that 1m will suit
runners.spotlight: 3yo who built upon his previous promise to win at Doncaster (7f, good to soft) in July; hit the front in the final furlong there so there can be optimism he'll be suited by 1m; respected up 4lb.

runners.owner: Adam Mills And Jonnie Delta Racing
runners.comment: Well beaten in Leger Legends race on Sunday, but five wins here and could bounce back
runners.spotlight: 10yo who was well beaten in the Leger Legends race on Sunday; however, he won two in a row at Hamilton this summer (1m, good) and is a five-time winner at Ayr (soft/good to soft); could bounce back with a bold show.

quotes.race: Bet At racingtv.com Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
quotes.quote: Ayr Poet was super and took me the full way. I just sat there and he did his job. I thought coming to the two-furlong pole that he's travelling very well and then I thought today might be the day for that first winner. Jim said let him jump and sit handy and if not, don't panic and he'd be good for me. There's no racing in my family at all and I'm continuing as an amateur as I'm a bit heavy for an apprentice licence, but Jim's been so generous in helping young people like me progress - Megan Davies, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

quotes.race: Tennent's Lager Handicap (Div II)
quotes.quote: I thought we had it on the line but we did it the tough way. Ayr Poet knows where the winning post is here. He's a good doer and is from a family we've done well with here, as he's the fourth winner out of his dam, with Euchen Glen and Sir Chauvelin both running this week too - Jim Goldie, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: His brother wasn't too bad as a 12-year-old and I'm sure he'd be quite good as a 13-year-old if I kept him going. He'll win more races, I wouldn't be keeping him going if I didn't think I could. It's been a very lucky family for me. 07-05-25

runners.comment: Below best at Haydock last time but has a strong record here and could play a leading role
runners.spotlight: Two C&D wins this season (good to firm/good) and runner-up over C&D (good to soft) two starts ago; also effective on soft ground; below best at Haydock last time but could play a leading role now back here.

quotes.race: racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap
quotes.quote: It's probably the place to be running today from the front. We were dropping Penelope's Sister back in trip and we know she gets this one well. Jim's [Goldie, trainer] horses are all healthy and in great form - Paul Mulrennan, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

prev_owners.owner: Gordon Bulloch & S Hardcastle D Szepler

runners.comment: Runner-up at Carlisle last September but she's struggled on her four starts this year
runners.spotlight: Runner-up at Carlisle (1m, soft) last September and now 4lb lower but she's struggled on her four starts this year (two hurdles, two Flat).

stable_tour.quote: She's a bit of a Beverley specialist. The ground is key to her, she loves it soft. She wants a stiff finish, so will be aimed at races at Pontefract and Beverley, and Hamilton would also suit her. This year she seems to be bigger and stronger and seems to have improved a lot. We haven't got a specific target in mind but the next few weeks are set to be quite dry, so we might just leave her for a bit. 21-06-24

runners.comment: Had been running well but not the last twice, including here on Thursday
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who had been running well but not the last twice, including here on Thursday.

racecards.verdict: The mare PENELOPE'S SISTER has a strong record here and can return to winning ways now back at Ayr, following a lesser run at Haydock.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 The Childe Of Hale, 5/1 Jewel Maker,  Penelope's Sister, 11/2 Temper Trap, 7/1 Pol Roger, 15/2 Arkleside, 9/1 Waiting All Night,  Ayr Poet, 25/1 Cavalry Call, 33/1 She's Got Bottle

racecards.race_name: RBC Brewin Dolphin Wealth Management Handicap (Div II)

runners.comment: Poor strike-rate and below best on last two starts but he's dropped down the weights
runners.spotlight: He's won just one of his 21 starts and hasn't been at the top of his game on his last two starts; has done the majority of his racing over further but trainer's horses are frequently versatile in terms of trip and he's dropped down the weights; not ruled out on second run back from a break.

quotes.race: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap (Div I)
quotes.quote: We got Sea Legend but he had a problem in behind so he had to have a long time off. We gelded him and the last month he's gone nicely. He enjoyed that trip, he's got a big, long stride and I'm really pleased with that on his first run back. It was a relief to go to the races and run so well. I think he'll be suited by a mile and a half and we'll look at all the options in those nice handicaps. That was his first run for a year, so we wanted to get it out the way before making plans - James Fanshawe, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's a nice, big horse. He's had problems but I'm hopefully quite good at fixing them. I'm looking forward to him, mainly for his pedigree. Sea The Stars was one of my favourite horses and he's good at producing top horses. 07-05-25

runners.comment: Needs to produce more than on her last two starts but went close over C&D previously
runners.spotlight: She needs to produce more than she's managed on her last two starts but before those she went close over C&D (good) and she won on soft/good to soft ground last September; capable of having a big say.

stable_tour.quote: She's fantastic - she's not won yet this season but was second in a big fillies' race at Musselburgh and second in a Sunday Series race at Hamilton so she's been paying her way. She likes a bit of rain, so I wouldn't be surprised to see her flourishing and winning a few races come September and October when we get a bit of soft ground. 17-07-25

prev_owners.owner: Dianne Sayer Anna Noble Arnie Flower

runners.comment: 11yo who has won here eight times but he's nothing like the force of old
runners.spotlight: 11yo who has won here eight times and has shown the odd sign of spark this season; continues to drop down the weights but his last three performances haven't indicated that a return to winning ways is imminent.

quotes.race: Virgin Bet Best Odds Daily Handicap
quotes.quote: Redarna is a wonderful little horse who tries his heart out, which he certainly did there. He's just fabulous. It's quite strange that he's now winning over seven [furlongs] when it always used to be eight ? he's gone the opposite way to most horses.  There's no race for him at the final meeting at Ayr, so he'll probably go to the grass now and we'll be back to give it another go next year - Dianne Sayer, trainer.

quotes.race: Blackwell Handicap (Div I)
quotes.quote: Redarna has run some good races here. I thought he needed soft ground so he's proved me wrong. The cheekpieces have helped him come out of the stalls as that was the best he has broke - Dianne Sayer, trainer.

quotes.race: Wemyss Bay Holiday Park Handicap (Div II)
quotes.quote: Redarna has run some good races at five and six furlongs so I wouldn't say he was necessarily any better at this trip. The ground was probably too quick for him last time and he was just that bit sharper today - Dianne Sayer, trainer.

quotes.race: Wemyss Bay Holiday Park Handicap (Div II)
quotes.quote: Redarna stayed on well over a stiff five furlongs at Carlisle last time and we thought bringing him here and trying him over seven furlongs we could learn a bit more about him. This is probably the right trip for him but the cut in the ground was also a help. It would be nice if we could find a suitable amateurs' race for him now, so that I can ride him! - Emma Sayer, trainer's daughter.

runners.comment: Lightly raced 3yo who could benefit from the drop back in trip on stable debut
runners.spotlight: Down the field for George Boughey in May/June in his first two handicaps and changed hands for 12,000gns in July; however, this 3yo was placed in 1m maiden/novice races in the spring and can go well if tapping back into that promise now back down in trip on this stable debut.

runners.comment: Disappointing in hat-trick bid last month but that run may have come too soon
runners.spotlight: Knocking on the door prior to winning at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) in July and she followed up at Leicester (1m, good to firm); only fifth of seven back at Doncaster last month but that may have come too soon, just six days after Leicester; this 3yo could rediscover her progressive ways today.

runners.breeder: Scea Marmion Vauville & Skymarc Farm Inc
runners.comment: Slow ground might be a positive but he was soundly beaten at Haydock only 12 days ago
runners.spotlight: Won on 2yo debut at Haydock (1m, soft) last September; the return to slow ground may be a positive but he's gone backwards from that initial promise and was soundly beaten at Haydock (1m, good) only 12 days ago.

stable_tour.quote: He's a Starspangledbanner but I think he'll stay. We overfaced him last year. He won on his debut but it was all a bit too much too soon on his next start in France. He's done well since and I'm sure he'll get a mile and a quarter, and perhaps a mile and a half. 09-04-25

runners.comment: Yet to win on turf but third in Sunday's Leger Legends race and could be in the mix
runners.spotlight: 0-11 on turf as opposed to 2-13 on AW but he ran pretty well when fifth in this race last year (good) and was third of 14 in the Leger Legends race on Sunday (1m, good to soft); could be in the mix.

runners.comment: Down the field on last four starts but drops back to 1m and is on his last winning mark
runners.spotlight: Down the field on his last four starts but those have been over 1m2f-1m4f and this 3yo is back down to the same mark as for his win at Southwell (1m, AW) in March; not written off now back down in distance.

runners.comment: Often runs well but not last time and has won just one of his 19 starts
runners.spotlight: 3yo who has often run well this year and holds each-way claims on his best form; however, he was below par last time and has won just one of his 19 starts, while he's unproven on slow ground.

racecards.verdict: The filly COEUR JAUNE looked a 3yo to follow prior to coming up short in last month's hat-trick bid, and that run may have come too soon
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/2 Coeur Jaune, 5/1 Quiet Resolve, 11/2 Epidavros, 13/2 Motawaared, 8/1 Redarna, 9/1 One Eye Jack, 10/1 Perfidia, 11/1 West Tyrone, 12/1 Sea Legend

racecards.race_name: Ladbrokes "Get Rewarded With Ladbucks" Handicap

runners.comment: Out of the money here on Thursday when behind Little Ted
runners.spotlight: Dual Irish winner who has been well held for this yard bar one run; out of the money here on Thursday when behind Little Ted.

runners.comment: Close third at Ayr in most recent 7f attempt; possibilities off last winning mark
runners.spotlight: Unsuited by drop to 6f last time; gradually back to form otherwise this term, close third at Ayr (good) in most recent 7f attempt; possibilities off last winning mark, provided he takes to added cheekpieces.

runners.comment: Consistent over 6f of late; place claims provided he's as effective back at 7f
runners.spotlight: Recorded last three wins over 6f at Catterick (good/soft); consistent at same trip of late and has place claims provided he's as effective back over 7f; distance winner a long time ago.

runners.comment: Attractively handicapped off 10lb below last winning mark; check the betting
runners.spotlight: Has edged down the weights this term for new yard and looks attractively handicapped off 10lb below last winning mark in 2023; all wins at 6f but has form over 7f; market support should be noted.

stable_tour.quote: He's really interesting. He came here with some foot problems, but our farriers have done an amazing job sorting that. He's got some solid sprint handicap form and his owner James Finch, who also has Andaleep, loves pot-hunting in the Sunday Series and the Racing League, so that's where we'll aim. 02-05-24

runners.breeder: J Hanly, Castlemartin Sky & Skymarc Far
runners.comment: Maiden; had wind surgery prior to encouraging effort last time; not dismissed
runners.spotlight: 3yo maiden who has proved inconsistent this season but may be about to turn over a new leaf, having had wind surgery prior to encouraging effort (close third) over the sharp 1m at Thirsk most recently; not dismissed.

stable_tour.quote: A really nice filly who ran a good race in third at Thirsk last month. She was a little bit keen early on and it just told in the later stages. It was her first time at a mile and she seemed to get the trip all right. She probably doesn't mind a bit of dig in the ground and she'll be winning before too long. 10-09-25

runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd, Cassie And Scott

prev_owners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

runners.comment: Knocking at the door for new yard; due to go up 2lb in future; enters calculations
runners.spotlight: Record is 7-65; has a touch of seconditis, including at Ayr, for new yard but by the same token he seems to be knocking at the door; ran well on AW last week and is due to go up 2lb in future; shortlisted.

stable_tour.quote: Ursa Major bought him at the sales earlier this month. He's a nice horse. He was at Mick Appleby's yard - he's been around a lot of yards and run a lot but always seems to do his share of winning. We picked him up to replace one (Jumeira Vision) that's just been retired in the last few weeks. 17-07-25

runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde
runners.comment: Continues his progressive year with a battling success over 7f here on Thursday
runners.spotlight: Generally progressive this year, showing improvement from 7f to 1m2f; continued his good work with a battling success here over the shorter trip on Thursday; penalised accordingly but hard to knock.

runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner; made the frame in last two runs; could go well
runners.spotlight: Record of 7-44 includes three wins over C&D (good to soft); hasn't strictly built on reappearance effort this season but is still in reasonable form and made the frame in last two runs; could go well.

stable_tour.quote: He's been running fairly well. He loves Ayr - he's won three times there and there's a race for him at the Western meeting next week. 10-09-25

runners.comment: Failed to revive when upped to 7f last time and remains a maiden
runners.spotlight: 3yo maiden who commenced her 2025 campaign with a couple of good sprinting efforts (AW/turf); not in the same form since, most recently upped to 7f; enough to prove.

runners.comment: Record of 4312 for new stable, the win over 7f; may do better still; respected
runners.spotlight: Consistent maiden as a 2yo for previous yard; record of 4312 for new stable, the win at Redcar (7f, good to firm); ran creditably at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) last time; may do better still; respected.

runners.owner: S Bulmer, P Hebdon & R Taylor
runners.comment: Maiden; chance largely depends on how well he responds to first-time headgear
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; ran respectably dropped to 7f at Haydock (good) most recently, albeit without improving; chance of getting off the mark largely depends on how well he responds to first-time headgear.

runners.comment: Eyecatching effort at Haydock last time; the least exposed contender; interesting
runners.spotlight: The least exposed contender in this field, having raced only four times; eyecatching fourth, amid prolonged traffic issues, in Haydock handicap (upped to 7f; good) most recently; interesting off the same mark and with Warren Fentiman taking off a bonus 5lb.

runners.comment: Maiden; would have a fighting chance if back to peak form (two close seconds)
runners.spotlight: 3yo maiden who has lacked consistency this term; would have a fighting chance if back to peak form, namely close seconds on AW last autumn and turf this spring.

runners.comment: Consistent; runner-up to one in form here on Thursday and that was at 7f; better at 1m
runners.spotlight: Consistent and ran another sound race in defeat when beating all bar an in-form opponent here on Thursday; that was at 7f and this trip should suit him better.

racecards.verdict: Unexposed filly MINSHAAR is taken to atone for her luckless Haydock run and get off the mark. We've Got This is second pick.
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/2 Dwindling Funds,  Minshaar, 11/2 Cooperation, 7/1 Who Wants Me,  We've Got This, 10/1 John L Sullivan,  Crocodile Power, 14/1 Jkr Cobbler, 16/1 Vince Lombardi,  Sherlock,  Little Ted, 20/1 Cavallo Pazzo,  Glorious Kitty,  Off Spin

racecards.race_name: Tennent's Lager Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.comment: Did well in top staying handicaps on Flat in 2021 but not seen since
runners.spotlight: Developed into a pretty smart staying handicapper on the Flat for Mick and David Easterby in 2021, finishing third in 2m4f handicaps at Royal Ascot and Glorious Goodwood; respectable fifth of 32 in the Cesarewitch at Newmarket (2m2f) in October of that year but not seen since; can only watch given the length of absence.

quotes.race: Oakmere Homes EBF Novice Stakes (Plus 10 Race)
quotes.quote: Elysian Flame ran well at Ayr on debut and knew more today. He loved the ground and is a horse to look forward to - Nathan Evans, jockey.

runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum
runners.comment: Well held in Royal Ascot Group 2 but won two novices prior to that; very interesting
runners.spotlight: Took novice events at Beverley (1m2f, good) in April and Yarmouth (11.5f, good to soft) in the spring; well held when pitched into the Group 2 Queen's Vase at Royal Ascot (1m6f, good to firm) in June but retains obvious unexposed potential now handicapping after three months off.

quotes.race: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
quotes.quote: Spinning Wheel is very tough and stays very well. He likes to get his toe in a bit and could be a nice horse as time goes on. He's done well to win under a 5lb penalty. The more you ask, the more he gives you, so he's a joy to ride - Harry Davies, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen
runners.comment: Well held in Melrose at York but won twice prior to that and could resume progression now
runners.spotlight: All five starts on good to firm, landing maiden and handicap wins around 1m4f in midsummer; disappointed when stepped up to 1m6f for the strong Melrose Handicap at the York Ebor meeting last month but good chance he can resume his progression back in much calmer waters.

runners.comment: Useful dual-purpose mare who was twice second on Flat before hurdle win in June; player
runners.spotlight: Two fine runner-up efforts in good-ground staying handicaps on the Flat at Ripon (2m) and Chester (2m2f) in the spring before returning to hurdles to win a Hexham handicap (good to soft) in June; won on heavy ground last September so further rain wouldn't be an issue; should go well for last year's winning yard.

quotes.race: High Gosforth Park Golf Club Mares' Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: Surrey Belle is a good filly, she's got a great cruising speed and loads of scope. When I bought her I had fences in mind.   She'll probably end up a 130-rated filly over hurdles and she'll be a really exciting novice chaser for next season.   We'll keep an eye on the weather at Cheltenham, when you go up into that type of class give in the ground would help her a lot, but she's also in the Herring Queen Series final at Kelso, that's a £100,000 race and gives me a bit more time after today so that's probably where she'll go next - Adrian Keatley, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She's just back in after a break. She's been brilliant for us, bar one off-day in a £100,000 race at Kelso when she was a bit flat going into it. We gave her a break after she won at Hexham last time and she'll go novice chasing. We'll probably give her a run or two on the Flat before the season ends as she handles soft ground. Chasing is what we're really looking at and we think she could be very good at it. We think she can be a big earner over the winter. She doesn't need to front-run but the way she attacks a fence and gallops relentlessly, I can see that playing to her strengths. 07-08-25

runners.comment: Second at Thirsk this month but only ninth at Doncaster since, taking her to 0-12 in 2015
runners.spotlight: Five wins in 2024 but none in 12 outings this season; her second at Thirsk (1m4f) this month was the closest she's come to her peak form this year but she could only manage ninth at Doncaster last week.

quotes.race: Racecourse Live Streams On Racing TV Extra Handicap
quotes.quote: Tafsir is a well-bred filly so it's good to get a win, but Paul's given her an absolute peach of a ride. He's gone down the brave man's route down the inside rail and has won quite comfortably in the end. She'll win again, especially with the assistance of Paul. He's top-class - Jim Goldie, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She's challenging but she always has been. It's always been onwards and upwards with her and she has her own way of doing things. She probably needs to race herself fit. She probably needs a couple of runs to get there. It could have been a blindfold issue on her penultimate start and she doesn't make it easy for the jockeys as she knows the rhythm of the stalls and she starts to mess about - she plays games. She couldn't make an impression behind Faylaq at Hamilton last time. She was probably the cheapest of Hamdan's cast-offs and it looked like they were right, but now she's won more races than most of them. He bred her to be better than 78, so we don't know if her mark is too high. 07-05-25

runners.comment: Won on soft one year ago but well held at Redcar in October and off since
runners.spotlight: Ended a losing run at Chester (1m4f, soft) last September but was only sixth at Redcar the following month and not seen since; no great record fresh and probably safe to look elsewhere.

stable_tour.quote: He was off for a long time and things haven't exactly worked out since he came back but I've always held him in high regard. His best run was last time at Hamilton, where he was held up off a nice gallop and finished well. I feel as though a win with him is imminent, at anything from 1m5f to 2m. He's dangerously handicapped. 26-07-24

racecards.verdict: Golden Handshake and SPINNING WHEEL appeal as 3yos who could get back on the up after lesser efforts in strong races.
racecards.betting_forecast: 13/8 Spinning Wheel, 5/2 Golden Handshake,  Surrey Belle, 11/1 Tafsir, 20/1 Elysian Flame,  Haveyoumissedme

racecards.race_name: Flutter Proudly Supporting Pancreatic Cancer UK Nursery Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.comment: Improved to win quite valuable Windsor novice (1m, soft) last month; AW/nursery debut
runners.spotlight: Placed twice over 7f before displaying improvement to win quite valuable Windsor novice (1m, soft) 17 days ago; needs to transfer that progress to Polytrack on this nursery debut but ought to be suited by today's trip.

runners.owner: N Bradley 29, Resdev & South Bank Racing

prev_owners.owner: N Bradley Racing 29 & South Bank Racing

runners.comment: 0-6 on turf but pretty consistent; upped from 7f and hooded for this AW debut
runners.spotlight: 0-6 on turf but scores pretty good marks for his consistency; not beaten far when fourth of six on recent nursery debut at Carlisle (7f, good), despite racing a bit too freely; hiked up in trip and sports first-time hood for this AW debut.

runners.comment: Won small-field AW nursery last month and ran well in defeat on turf last week
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive colt who won small-field Kempton nursery (1m) last month, his fifth run, and was creditable third in quite valuable event at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) last week; up in trip here; good chance.

racecards.verdict: Preference is for ROYAL BODYGUARD, who followed last month's small-field win on this surface with a creditable third on turf last week.
racecards.betting_forecast: Evs Royal Bodyguard, 7/4 Explosion, 4/1 The Resdev Scholar

racecards.race_name: Betfair "Hands And Heels" Amateur Jockeys' Handicap

runners.comment: Came good at tenth attempt, with C&D win six weeks ago; makes strong appeal
runners.spotlight: Came good at tenth attempt, winning ten-runner handicap over C&D six weeks ago; 5lb higher today but still makes strong appeal in a modest race.

runners.comment: Held on well for narrow Newcastle win in June; easily excused recent defeat on heavy turf
runners.spotlight: Displayed improvement when fitted with blinkers for his two runs in June, latterly when holding on narrowly to open account at Newcastle (1m2f); recent defeat on heavy turf can easily be overlooked; first ride under rules for Liam McGaffin; one to consider.

runners.comment: Both runs for new connections this summer were disappointing; drops back in trip today
runners.spotlight: Scored off today's mark on turf (1m2f, good to firm) for Ismail Mohammed in May but both runs in amateur riders' races for new connections this summer were disappointing, latterly when racing far too freely in the lead over 1m6f in a first-time visor; drops back in trip and goes without headgear here but still looks risky.

runners.comment: Won off much reduced mark in January but well held on both subsequent outings
runners.spotlight: Just did enough to exploit reduced mark at Lingfield (1m2f) in January but his C&D run in March was disappointing and he ought to have coped much better with the good to soft turf on latest outing in July (tailed off).

runners.comment: Well-beaten 100-1 shot after blowing the start on recent stable/handicap debut
runners.spotlight: Still unexposed after only four starts but was a well-beaten 100-1 shot after blowing the start on recent stable/handicap debut at Lingfield (1m); upped in trip with cheekpieces added here; tongue-tie removed.

runners.comment: Tapeta regular; ran well on turf last month and remains on a workable mark
runners.spotlight: Tapeta regular who has raced mainly over 1m4f/1m6f on that surface this year; coped well with drop back to 1m2f when close third of five at Yarmouth (good to firm) last month, though, and remains on a workable mark; first ride under rules for Venetia Thompson.

quotes.race: Build Your Acca With BetUK Handicap
quotes.quote: I'm delighted with Prince Hector, he's taken a bit of getting to know but hopefully we can now keep his head in front - Jessica Bedi, trainer.

runners.comment: Well held last time but ran well on last month's stable debut; has most experienced jockey
runners.spotlight: Well held after racing too freely at Haydock (11.6f) recently and now 0-20 but ran quite well when fourth under Lauren Frost on last month's stable debut at Pontefract (1m2f, good to firm), and his jockey (three wins from 35 rides under rules) is by far the most experienced in this race.

runners.owner: Dr M Voikhansky & Mrs S Voikhanskaya
runners.comment: Yet to add to turf nursery win in 2022 but last month's Leicester fourth offers some hope
runners.spotlight: Yet to add to his turf nursery win three years ago but kept on for fourth off basement mark at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) in August and did not enjoy the run of the race in 1m4f AW classified last time; considered each-way.

quotes.race: Fairfax Saddles Grassroots Series Final Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: I spent most of the middle part of the race taking a pull on Pending Appeal and trying to fill her up. She's a quick filly and I maybe went 50 yards too soon, but she just did enough to hold on. Six furlongs is her limit at this stage and the nice ground suited her well - David Egan, jockey.

racecards.verdict: The most obvious answer is SENSE OF REASON, who won a slightly stronger C&D race than this when last in action six weeks ago.
racecards.betting_forecast: 13/8 Sense Of Reason, 7/2 Wyld Bill, 9/2 Prince Hector, 8/1 Mass Consumption, 10/1 Major Major,  Pending Appeal, 16/1 Hawa Jumeirah, 40/1 Henrytheninth

racecards.race_name: Bet At Your Best With Betfair Selling Stakes

runners.comment: Not favoured by the weights but has held his form well since two turf wins in May
runners.spotlight: Won two 1m2f turf handicaps in May and has held his form pretty well since; drops back in trip after looking stretched by 1m4f three weeks ago; not favoured by the weights but likely to give another good account.

runners.comment: Comfortable winner of quite valuable Goodwood seller last month; should be hard to beat
runners.spotlight: Delivered on promise shown in turf maidens when comfortably winning quite valuable Goodwood seller (1m3f, good to firm) last month; will be hard to beat here if handling the switch to Polytrack.

runners.comment: Good claims if judged on Doncaster second in July; dropped out tamely last month
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; posted best form when close second in Class 4 Doncaster handicap (1m2f, good) in July and has good claims if judged on that performance but dropped out as though something was amiss at Windsor last month; cheekpieces removed after one use; AW debut.

stable_tour.quote: He ran well last week at Doncaster and also first time at Redcar, finishing second each time, but not so much at Chester in between. However, Chester isn't for every horse and he didn't seem very comfortable around there. He stays 1m2f well and we're hoping to get his head in front sooner rather than later. He's in good form and is a tough little horse who wants to try hard, so there's no reason why he shouldn't win soon. 23-07-25

runners.comment: Placed on handicap debut in June but well held twice since, latterly in a seller
runners.spotlight: Placed on handicap debut at Ffos Las (1m6f, soft) in June, form that gives him a good chance on the figures, but two disappointing efforts have followed, the latter when over 17l behind Forever Penywern in 1m3f Goodwood seller.

racecards.verdict: It's hard to get away from FOREVER PENYWERN, who had a fair bit left in the tank when winning a pretty good seller last month.
racecards.betting_forecast: 4/9 Forever Penywern, 5/1 Divine Legacy, 11/2 Antelope, 14/1 Wise Counsellor

racecards.race_name: Betfair EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C and D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div I)

runners.comment: Made winning debut at Kempton and subsequent defeat came in a valuable sales race
runners.spotlight: Well-bred colt who kept on well to make winning debut at Kempton (7f, AW) last month; safely held in valuable sales race since but back in calmer waters here and has a big role to play.

runners.comment: Closely related to two very useful performers; has fairly good standard to reach on debut
runners.spotlight: 32,000euros yearling; ninth foal; closely related to an Italian Listed winner and Listed-placed 7f-1m winner (including 2yo) Mutamaiezahinc, half-brother to four other winners; dam 7f AW 2yo winner (RPR 74); has fairly good standard to reach on debut.

runners.comment: Will find this easier than York last month and has claims if judged on Lingfield second
runners.spotlight: Well down the field in valuable York nursery last month but will find this race easier and has claims if judged on his second from off the pace at Lingfield (7f, good to soft) in July; AW debut.

runners.comment: First foal from unraced half-sister to UAE dirt Group 1 winner; market helpful on debut
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam unraced sister to 1m3f-13.3f AW winner Fox Flame, half-sister to UAE 1m2f dirt Group 1 winner Salute The Soldier; market helpful on debut.

runners.comment: Displayed promise when fifth on first two starts but needs improvement
runners.spotlight: Displayed clear promise when fifth on first two outings (both 7f) but has a bit to find with the principals and will probably be of greater interest when qualified for nurseries.

runners.breeder: R F And S D Knipe
runners.comment: 17,000euros 2yo; half-brother to four winnners, one at Listed level; market instructive
runners.spotlight: 17,000euros breeze-up 2yo; ninth foal; half-brother to four winners including Global Applause (5f including 2yo/Listed; RPR 109), Revelstoke (German 6.5f 2yo/7f; 104) and Auditor (5f-7f including 2yo/Qatar; 98); dam 5f/6f winner (including 2yo/AW; 88); tongue tied on debut; market instructive.

runners.comment: Always towards rear on first two starts (6f/7f); cannot be recommended
runners.spotlight: Always towards rear on first two starts (6f/7f); cannot be recommended.

runners.comment: Made promising debut when second at Yarmouth last month; leading contender
runners.spotlight: Made promising debut at Yarmouth (7f, good to firm) last month, staying on from the rear to finish second of ten; leading contender, assuming the switch to Polytrack suits.

runners.comment: Not disgraced on debut, given she raced wide and hung on the home turn; still looks risky
runners.spotlight: From a good family and wasn't disgraced when eight of 12 on last month's debut at Kempton, given she lost ground when racing wide and hanging left on the home turn; can improve but looks risky from stall 11.

runners.comment: 21,000euros yearling; dam fair 7f winner; not ruled out on debut
runners.spotlight: 21,000euros yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to winners Karmology (Listed-placed 1m/1m2f including AW; RPR 102) and Muveran (1m AW; 65); dam 7f winner (75), half-sister to Group-placed 7.5f 2yo winner On Verra; not ruled out on debut.

runners.comment: Only 6-1 for 7f Lingfield maiden last month but was well beaten
runners.spotlight: Out of an unraced mare from a successful Hong Kong family; only 6-1 for 7f Lingfield maiden on last month's debut but was well beaten; needs to take a huge step forward in what looks a better race here.

racecards.verdict: Normal improvement ought to suffice for ROSA INGLESA, who kept on well for second on last month's debut at Yarmouth.
racecards.betting_forecast: 13/8 Rosa Inglesa, 5/2 Piscean Star, 11/2 Norfolk Blue, 12/1 Onyeisi,  Trust No One, 16/1 Maskatto, 20/1 Splash,  Karmacy,  Red Eve, 33/1 Marseillan, 66/1 Calypso Breeze

racecards.race_name: Betfair EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C and D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race) (Div II)

runners.owner: J Sarkar, D Sarkar, O Sarkar & D Sarkar
runners.comment: Promising fourth of five in what was probably a good 6f Ascot novice this month
runners.spotlight: Brother to useful 7f performer Great Acclaim; displayed clear promise when fourth of five in what was probably a good Ascot novice (6f, soft) this month; drawn wide here but can pose a threat if this very different C&D suits.

runners.comment: Put in fairly good front-running shift before fading into fifth on recent debut
runners.spotlight: Represents stable not known for 2yo winners and was 50-1 for recent debut at Lingfield (7f) but put in a fairly good front-running shift before fading into fifth and evidently has ability.

runners.comment: Safely held over on debut in April and not seen again since; improvement possible, though
runners.spotlight: First foal from a triple Group 3 winner (6f-1m2f); safely held when fourth of five on debut at Ayr (5f, good) in April and not seen again since but that form looks better now than it did at the time; open to improvement; market may guide.

runners.comment: Never really got competitive (albeit on soft turf) on recent debut
runners.spotlight: £34,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; raced too freely and never really got competitive when eighth of ten on debut at Windsor (1m, soft) this month; significant improvement required.

runners.comment: Slowly away, always behind and beaten about 49l when 66-1 for debut at Windsor (6f)
runners.spotlight: Slowly away, always behind and beaten about 49l when 66-1 for debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm) in June; gelded during subsequent 12-week break.

runners.comment: Stable has only occasional debut winners and this colt will probably need further than 7f
runners.spotlight: 14th foal; half-brother to seven winners, most notably middle-distance Group 2 winner Worth Waiting; dam French 12.5f-1m7f winner; stable has only occasional debut winners and this colt probably won't be seen to best effect until tackling longer trips.

runners.comment: With a top stable and does not face a daunting task on debut; must be considered
runners.spotlight: 30,000gns yearling; eighth foal; half-sister to five US winners; dam 6f winner (RPR 74), half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Enticing; with a top stable and does not face a daunting task on debut; must be considered.

runners.comment: 10,000gns yearling; bred to be a speedy 2yo; a newcomer with possibilities on paper
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns yearling; ninth foal; half-sister to five winners including Windstormblack (5f/6f including Italian 2yo Group 3; RPR 112), and Francisco's Piece (5f 2yo including Listed; 99); dam 5f AW winner (71); a newcomer with possibilities on paper.

runners.comment: Sister to a Swedish 1m 2yo winner; would be a rare first-time-out winner for the stable
runners.spotlight: £10,000 yearling; eighth foal; sister to a Swedish 1m 2yo winner, half-sister to winners Abu Malek (6f; RPR 82) and Winning Together (Hong Kong 7f); dam unraced half-sister to 6f Group 3 winner Hitchens; would be a rare first-time-out winner for the stable.

runners.comment: Ran well for a long way on debut but dropped out tamely on second outing; now hooded
runners.spotlight: Led for a long way before fading into a respectable fifth at Kempton (7f) last month but dropped out as though something was amiss at Lingfield a fortnight later; now hooded; has a point to prove.

runners.comment: Well beaten when 40-1 for last month's Lingfield debut (7f)
runners.spotlight: Well beaten when 40-1 for last month's Lingfield debut (7f) and difficult to enthuse over after that performance.

racecards.verdict: George Scott's ACCLAIMED FREEDOM shaped pretty well on soft ground at Ascot a fortnight ago and gets the vote.
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/4 Acclaimed Freedom, 11/4 Athena's War, 5/1 Mick From Tipp, 6/1 Mr Nippy, 8/1 Baandee, 14/1 Onwardupward, 20/1 The Wicked Wolf, 25/1 Timely Salute,  Bellini Spirit, 40/1 Virginia Moon, 100/1 Jooliah

racecards.race_name: Betfair Safer Gambling Tools Nursery Handicap

runners.owner: P C J Dalby & R D Schuster
runners.comment: Kept on for fourth of seven when upped to 7f for nursery debut; can feature
runners.spotlight: Looked suited by the step up to 7f when keeping-on fourth of seven on nursery debut at Kempton; 1lb lower and eased slightly in grade; can make his presence felt.

stable_tour.quote: If you're looking for one for some instant gratification, he'll probably start over 6f and be out quite soon. His sire won the Coventry in 2020 at 150-1 on just his second start. 30-04-25

runners.comment: Safely held when third of four last month and now 0-7; needs to up his game
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden who was safely held when third of four in Brighton nursery (5.2f, good to firm) last month; probably worth another crack at 7f but needs to up his game.

runners.comment: Soundly beaten over 6f here on nursery debut; upped in trip with cheekpieces added tonight
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten after making the running on nursery debut here (6f; jockey said filly ran green) three weeks ago; moves up in trip and sports first-time cheekpieces tonight.

runners.comment: Respectable third over C&D last month but this quirky filly comes with risk
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden; kept on well for a respectable third behind Captain Bruce over C&D last month but this quirky filly needs to learn to settle better and comes with risk.

runners.comment: Kept on well to score by 2l over C&D last month; up 6lb but still an obvious contender
runners.spotlight: Always behind when 50-1 for last month's nursery debut but was an entirely different proposition over C&D a fortnight later, keeping on well to score by 2l; 6lb higher today but still an obvious contender.

runners.comment: Not disgraced in C&D novice last month; ought to be much more competitive tonight
runners.spotlight: Unable to land a significant blow in his three qualifying races but was not disgraced when fifth of nine in fairly good C&D novice three weeks ago and ought to be much more competitive in this low-grade nursery.

runners.comment: Not beaten far in first two nurseries (5f/7f) and recent 3lb drop helps
