runners.spotlight: Not beaten far in her first two nurseries, more recently when fifth (just behind Fanjove) at Kempton (7f) four weeks ago; still unexposed over this trip and recent 3lb drop helps; no surprise if she features.

runners.comment: Soundly beaten in all three qualifying races (7f-1m) but a possible improver in handicaps
runners.spotlight: Out of a very useful 6f/7f performer; did not live up to her pedigree when soundly beaten in all three qualifying races (7f-1m) but improvement is possible in handicaps.

runners.comment: Well down the field in 6f maiden/novice races; may fare better in low-grade nurseries
runners.spotlight: Well down the field in three 6f maiden/novice races this summer but ought to be suited by this extra furlong and is another who may fare better now switched to a low-grade nursery.

runners.comment: Ducked left at start and was soon struggling when 66-1 for recent nursery debut
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after only four runs but ducked sharply left at start and was soon struggling on recent nursery debut at Beverley (7.4f; 66-1); sports cheekpieces today; big leap of faith required to support him.

racecards.verdict: C&D winner Captain Bruce is respected but nursery debutant THANH NAM may improve past him.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/2 Captain Bruce, 11/2 Fanjove, 6/1 Thanh Nam,  Miss Ayala, 7/1 Imso Friendly,  Hoe Benham, 12/1 Archibald Ives,  Carefree, 20/1 Molly Mac, 40/1 Bestfootleads

racecards.race_name: Betfair Plays Different Handicap

runners.comment: Third over 1m4f on AW latest and won only Chelmsford start; claims if stamina holds
runners.spotlight: 7-25 on the AW, with one of the wins coming on his only Chelmsford start; creditable third of ten at Lingfield (1m4f, AW) 19 days ago and the second from that race has won since; stamina not assured over the longest he's tackled but can be very competitive from his mark.

quotes.race: Huge Daily Boosts Only At BetUK Handicap
quotes.quote: Obsidian Knight has won round here three times now but I wouldn't say he has to come here. What he needs is a proper gallop, and he got one today. We're looking forward to running him on the turf, but whether we give him a break or go for one of the bonuses, I'll speak to the owners and see what they want to do. He seems to handle the track lovely and Tom gave him an absolutely perfect ride - Terry Kent, trainer.

runners.owner: Mrs S Russell & A M Russell
runners.comment: Good second over 1m5f on AW latest and can go well again
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who was in good form in midsummer, winning twice at around 1m6f on turf in June; back from a couple of lesser efforts when second over 1m5f at Lingfield (AW) 15 days ago and can go well again.

runners.comment: Arrives in form and excellent record at Chelmsford (5-6) puts him top of the shortlist
runners.spotlight: Won five times here (1m6f/2m between December and May; also won a four-runner novice handicap hurdle at Southwell (2m) in June; runner-up back on Flat (turf) in July/August and also a creditable third over hurdles 18 days ago; his excellent Chelmsford record puts him at the top of the shortlist with Mason Paetel taking 5lb off.

stable_tour.quote: Freddy Tylicki sourced him privately off Tracy Waggott for me. He's owned by some friends of mine. He's two from two for us and we'll now probably sit and wait and have a good look at one of these bonuses with him in January. I still think he's got plenty of potential and, being by Frankel, he's a strong stayer. He's just a good, sound horse. He's run once over hurdles and we'll go hurdling with him again over the summer. 01-01-25

runners.comment: First handicap start on AW when second at Southwell (1m4f) latest; thereabouts with repeat
runners.spotlight: 0-9 but did bounce back from a lesser effort at Sandown (1m6f) when second at Southwell (1m4f) last month in his first handicap on AW; thereabouts with a repeat.

runners.comment: In form since visored, winning on turf and second on AW latest; another to consider
runners.spotlight: Got back on the up when landing 11.5f handicap at Yarmouth last month and just as good when second on AW at Southwell (1m4f) 16 days ago; up in trip; another to consider.

runners.comment: No extra late on when third over 1m6f here latest so slightly shorter trip could be ideal
runners.spotlight: Winner at Haydock in May (1m4f) and mostly creditable efforts since, including third over 1m6f here last week; no extra towards the finish on that occasion so this evening's slightly shorter trip could be ideal.

racecards.verdict: A case can be made for all of these, with CARLTON's excellent Chelmsford record earning him the vote. Curran is second choice.
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 Kingmaker, 7/2 Carlton, 9/2 Curran,  Telecommunication, 11/2 Obsidian Knight, 6/1 Is I Right

racecards.race_name: betting.betfair.com Handicap

runners.owner: A Bangs & Miss J A Challen
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner who won at Leicester in July and didn't get any luck last time; in the mix
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner who made all when scoring at Leicester in July and he had lots of traffic issues when ridden more patiently at Windsor (seventh of nine) last time; still looks on a workable mark and he's respected back on AW.

runners.comment: 2lb lower than for his last win but he's lost his way and needs a major revival
runners.spotlight: Three wins at Wolverhampton early last year and added another success at Kempton (6f, Polytrack) in February; now 2lb lower, but he will need to leave his last two efforts behind if he is to capitalise.

runners.comment: Refused to race at Yarmouth last time and is now 0-16 since her sole win last spring
runners.spotlight: Did well to finish a close third after missing the break at Yarmouth (5f, good) in June but she refused to race there last month and is now 0-16 since her sole win last spring.

runners.comment: On dangerous mark but she needs first-time blinkers to make a difference
runners.spotlight: Won with cheekpieces added on stable debut at Lingfield (5f, AW) in February but she's not finished closer than fourth in ten subsequent starts; now 8lb lower than for her win but she needs first-time blinkers to make a difference.

runners.comment: 0-8 but he wasn't beaten far here last time and could be dangerous back in trip
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but he was only beaten 2l in a 7f handicap here last month; continues to drop down the weights and could be dangerous if he can find something extra back in trip.

runners.comment: 0-15 but she had a near-miss here (5f) on her penultimate run; in the mix
runners.spotlight: 15-race maiden but she had a near-miss here (5f) last month and wasn't beaten far on her return visit last week; should be fine on this step back up in trip and she's only 1lb higher than for her penultimate run; possibilities.

runners.comment: 15-race maiden and he's finished down the field in last two starts; down the list
runners.spotlight: Reappeared with a second at Lingfield (7f, AW) in June but he's finished down the field in both subsequent runs; pulled hard last time and now drops back in trip but he's still a maiden after 15 starts; others more convincing.

runners.comment: Fair efforts on Polytrack last twice but she needs to find more to make the breakthrough
runners.spotlight: On reduced mark and she's run respectably at Lingfield (6f, AW) in last two starts but record is now 0-14 and she needs to find more.

runners.owner: D Morgan And K J Parris
runners.comment: Easy win at Wolverhampton on Tuesday and she's a major player again under penalty
runners.spotlight: Frm figures of 242421 since joining Martin Dunne and that win was by 4l from a wide draw at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) on Tuesday; looks well treated under a penalty and she ran well over C&D on penultimate run; major player.

runners.comment: Hasn't been with James Owen for long and he's finished second in last two runs; respected
runners.spotlight: 21-race maiden but he's not been with James Owen for long and has finished runner-up in classified events over C&D and at Lingfield (7f, AW) in last two starts; should get a good pace on this drop back in trip and he's respected back in a handicap; reapplied cheekpieces replace a visor.

runners.comment: Disappointing this season but he's now 10lb lower than when winning this race last year
runners.spotlight: Won this race last year off 10lb higher mark and was a close third over C&D in March but he's been disappointing since and was tailed off at Lingfield (7f, soft) last time; comes with risks attached but this drop back in trip is a positive.

runners.comment: 11-race maiden who has struggled last twice and is untried at this trip; others preferred
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden; didn't get much luck when third at Lingfield (7f, good to soft) in July but she's finished out the back on AW there in both subsequent runs; has quite a lot to prove again and she's untried at this trip.

runners.comment: Struggled in her qualifying runs and same story on handicap debut at Southwell (7f, AW)
runners.spotlight: Struggled in her three qualifying runs and same story on handicap debut at Southwell (7f, AW) two weeks ago; still early days but she needs a transformation back in trip.

racecards.verdict: This can go to SAM'S HOPE who won by 4l at Wolverhampton on Tuesday and looks well treated under a penalty.

runners.spotlight: Out of a Lancashire Oaks (and AW) winner for his connections; last of ten in soft-ground event at Sandown but may do better on this surface with the outing under his belt.

runners.comment: Gives the impression he'll find better opportunities in handicaps soon

We got an easy lead and I kicked plenty early enough as I know he keeps galloping. I know him well and on other horses you might be a bit worried about kicking that early, but I know he's a tough boy - Adam Farragher, jockey, speaking on Racing TV.

runners.comment: Has failed to back up the form of her Windsor win; opposed

quotes.quote: I'd say He's Waliim is a very good horse. He gave me a lovely ride, was professional and I was impressed with his mannerisms when I got on him. James said to me that he was a lovely horse and he liked him a lot. He was ready to run [in June] but they just had to back off because of his shins. They sort of left me in front so I went steady and caught them on the back foot. Had I gone on the stride I don't think they would have been able to come with me anyway - Clifford Lee, rider, speaking to Racing TV.

stable_tour.quote: He should have made his debut at Sandown a couple of weeks ago but he had a small issue and we had to cancel that. He'd been showing up well on the gallops before and should be one to follow. 09-07-25

runners.spotlight: Made a promising debut when second of 11 at Doncaster (7f, good) in July and looked smart when bolting up by nearly 10l in a novice over C&D (4-11) last month; major player.

I was surprised we could buy him at the price we got him for - Mick Mariscotti, joint owner, speaking on Racing TV.

runners.spotlight: All four starts this season over C&D, going close on reappearance; best run since when third of six 43 days ago and has dipped to a mark in the 60s for the first time since a Chester win in 2023; could go well.

quotes.race: MansionBet Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: That was a disaster. I left the ear plugs in and Noel said Capone never woke up. He's going to be a much nicer horse next year and it's great to give Noel a winner - Charlie Mann, trainer.

quotes.race: Best Wishes For 2019 Juvenile Hurdle
quotes.quote: Noel (Fehily) thinks I ran Capone too soon as he didn't settle today but he's a lot better than that. He won't run again for at least a month but he's a decent horse. He works very well and we've got nothing that can go with him at home - Charlie Mann, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He was a present from Charlie Mann when he retired. He's had a wonderful season but sadly he irritated an old leg injury when I ran him at Salisbury the other day. He couldn't get into the race on the rail and when he got clear he stretched and I think he did something funny when he crossed the road, so we won't see him in a little while. 21-09-23

runners.comment: Struggled on Flat earlier in year only sixth back hurdling for new trainer 13 days ago
runners.spotlight: Struggled on the Flat for Anthony Charlton earlier in the year and could only manage sixth of eight in a 2m4f Worcester handicap hurdle 13 days ago on  first run for Syd Hosie; a first-time visor worn at Worcester is retained.

runners.comment: No impact in two handicaps but showed promise before that and Oisin Murphy rides
runners.spotlight: Promise in his qualifying runs but no impact in handicaps at Sandown (1m6f) in June and, having been gelded, over 1m3f here (visored) 22 days ago; still early days, though, and Oisin Murphy rides.

runners.comment: Beaten 5l when third on 2m AW handicap debut and needs to step forward
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced but the fact he was beaten 5l into third on his 2m Lingfield (AW) handicap debut 14 days ago shows his mark demands improvement.

runners.breeder: S C E A Haras Du Hoguenet Et Al
runners.comment: Well held on turf latest but seems to save best for AW and is well treated on peak form
runners.spotlight: Well held on soft turf a fortnight ago but seems to save his best for AW, including runner-up at Wolverhampton (1m6f) and Lingfield (2m) in the spring; penultimate fourth of 13 back at Lingfield was also respectable and he's well treated on peak form.

runners.comment: Well held in three turf handicaps but did hint at promise on AW at start of career
runners.spotlight: From a good yard but has struggled in three turf handicaps; did hint at promise in his qualifying runs on AW and needs the return to Polytrack to give him something extra.

runners.owner: O T I Racing, Paul Inglett & Partner
runners.comment: Well held on 1m4f turf handicap debut in cheekpieces; headgear off and up 4f in trip now
runners.spotlight: Disappointing start to his handicap career with cheekpieces added when last of seven at Ffos Las (1m4f) five weeks ago; the headgear is quickly discarded now stepping up significantly in trip.

runners.comment: 22l third when last seen in June but worth a betting check now handicapping after wind op
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in three qualifying runs over 1m2f/1m4f in May/June, including 22l last of three at Salisbury on the final one; has had wind surgery ahead of this handicap debut after a 100-day break; not bred to be a stayer but is a half-brother to some useful sorts and needs a betting check in a race where nothing arrives with compelling claims.

runners.comment: Well held on heavy-ground handicap debut over 11.5f; takes a big step up in trip
runners.spotlight: Failed to cut much ice in AW middle-distance maiden/novice contests in the spring and well held on heavy-gound Windsor handicap debut (11.5f)) on return from a break 17 days ago; another who takes a big step up in trip.

racecards.verdict: This looks trappy to say the least. SYDNEY SEABREEZE has saved his best for AW so might be worth chancing from a reduced mark.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 St Mawes, 5/1 Warrior King, 11/2 Capone,  Sydney Seabreeze, 6/1 Rogue Empire, 7/1 Bedford, 9/1 Navalanche, 12/1 Witch Hazel, 16/1 See The Green

racecards.race_name: Try Unibet's New Acca Boosts Handicap (London Middle Distance Series Qualifier)

runners.comment: Ended a losing run at Lingfield last time; stamina will be tested up again in trip
runners.spotlight: Produced a telling turn of foot to prevail in a bunch finish in a slowly run contest at Lingfield (1m2f, Polytrack) 15 days ago, ending a 21-race losing run; only 2lb higher, but steps up in trip again so his stamina will be tested further, especially if this is more truly run.

quotes.race: SOS Systems Handicap
quotes.quote: Royal Pleasure has always been a talented horse, but his nerves get the better of him. Cieren gave a fantastic and brave ride, the horse had cleared off twice before and it's a big ask to get a jockey up on them again - Karen Jewell, trainer.

runners.comment: Behind Royal Pleasure at Lingfield last time but these conditions may suit him better
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 21st attempt in an amateur riders' race at Chelmsford (1m2f, Polytrack) last month, but only fifth of nine behind Royal Pleasure at Lingfield (1m2f, Polytrack) 15 days ago; these conditions may suit him better having been placed in one go over this C&D.

stable_tour.quote: He's a nice colt who has done well from two to three. His three starts at two came over a mile, but he'll get a trip this season. 29-04-24

runners.comment: Four course wins; race not run to suit when behind two of these at Lingfield last time
runners.spotlight: Four-time course winner, including two over this trip; finished behind two of these at Lingfield (1m2f, Polytrack) 15 days ago, but that race wasn't run to suit him; no surprise if he turns the tables back at this track.

stable_tour.quote: He got the season off to a fantastic start by winning three races on the bounce, which saw his rating shoot up from 47 to 68. I then made the mistake of giving him time off to strengthen up and I haven't been happy with his last three runs. I expected a big show at Wolverhampton last time but he was mid-division. I've been happy with him at home of late and I think you'll see a different horse next time. 23-09-22

runners.comment: Finished last on stable/handicap debut at Lingfield 15 days ago; enough to prove
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in three AW novices for Andrew Balding last winter including over this C&D, but having been gelded he finished last of seven on stable/handicap debut at Lingfield (1m, Polytrack) 15 days ago; may have needed it, but still has enough to prove.

runners.comment: Just 1lb above last winning mark, but his last two efforts leave plenty to be desired
runners.spotlight: All five wins on the AW and just 1lb higher than when successful at Wolverhampton (1m4f, Tapeta) in February, but his last two efforts leave plenty to be desired; needs a resurgence back down in trip.

runners.comment: Has the form to figure, but twice proved reluctant to race during the summer
runners.spotlight: Returned to winning form when beating Solanna at Lingfield (1m4f, Polytrack) in June and ran a solid race when second of six back there ten days ago; shortlisted on those efforts, but twice proved reluctant to race during the summer so does come with risks attached.

runners.comment: Makes his stable debut in a first-time hood; worth monitoring in the market
runners.spotlight: Won over this trip at Hamilton (good) for Daragh Bourke in June, but they didn't go quick enough for him when fourth of eight back there (good) the following month; makes his stable debut in a first-time hood after 68 days off and worth monitoring in the market.

runners.comment: Won at Goodwood in June; just held at Newbury last month; cheekpieces on; holds claims
runners.spotlight: Won at Goodwood (1m2f, good) in June and went close off 1lb lower at Newbury (1m2f, good) last month (heavy ground may not have suited last time); Tapeta winner a year ago and has run well on Polytrack; each-way claims with cheekpieces given a go.

stable_tour.quote: He won a Goodwood handicap in June off 66 and went to Newbury on Friday for a 1m2f apprentice handicap, finishing a half-length second with five lengths back to the third. It was a good run off a mark of 68. He's a reliable sort whose best distance is a mile and a quarter and he'll keep going as long as there's some give in the ground. 20-08-25

runners.comment: Just beaten on her return here in April, but her form has regressed since; blinkers on
runners.spotlight: Went down narrowly on her return in a maiden over C&D in April, but since being fitted with a tongue-tie her form has regressed; blinkers given a go this time and they need to make a difference.

prev_owners.owner: Mohamed Jaber Alharbi & Rabbah Bloodstock Ltd

runners.comment: Very disappointing on handicap debut last month; interesting to see if her backers return
runners.spotlight: In the frame in her first two starts for this yard at Chelmsford (1m2f, Polytrack) and Nottingham (1m2f, good) in the summer, but was very disappointing on handicap debut at Lingfield (1m4f, Polytrack; 3-1) last month; interesting to see if her backers return.

runners.comment: Went close at Wolverhampton in April, but has regressed since and now 0-12
runners.spotlight: Placed twice here (1m) around this time last year and went close off 2lb higher in a first-time visor at Wolverhampton (9.5f, Tapeta) in April, but has regressed since and now 0-12; others stronger.

prev_owners.owner: John Rhodes Peter Timmins & Dale Wilsdon

runners.comment: 1lb lower than when winning narrowly over C&D in June; may be worth a second look
runners.spotlight: Gained a narrow success over C&D for Michael Wigham in June, but not matched that form in three starts since including two for this yard; now 1lb lower than for that latest success, though, so may be worth a second look in the returning tongue-tie.

runners.comment: Looked worth a go over this far when fourth at Chelmsford last time; one for the shortlist
runners.spotlight: Doesn't help himself with slow starts, but he overcame that when winning at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Tapeta) in June; looked worth a go over this far when a staying-on fourth of eight at Chelmsford (1m2f, Polytrack) four weeks ago; one for the shortlist if things drop right.

racecards.verdict: This can go to dual C&D winner SOLANNA who didn't have the race run to suit when behind two of these at Lingfield last time.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/1 Royal Pleasure, 6/1 Meleri, 7/1 Fihrayn,  Catherine's Gift,  Blue Train, 8/1 Solanna,  Bownder, 10/1 Redondo, 14/1 Three Yorkshiremen, 16/1 Upepo,  Lieutenant Lily,  Must Believe, 20/1 Nahy

racecards.race_name: Unibet Supports Safer Gambling Handicap

runners.comment: 7f win at Wolverhampton when last seen in April; 5lb higher today; stable run two here
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the tenth attempt when justifying favouritism at Wolverhampton (7f) in April; returns from a five-month break off a 5lb higher mark; stable also run Soar Above and the betting should provide clues.

runners.comment: Comes here in good form but 0-10 on AW and she has a tricky draw to contend with
runners.spotlight: She had dropped a long way in the weights prior to her easy win at Windsor in July (6f, good); two solid efforts followed last month and although she's 0-10 on AW, she has run well over C&D; tricky draw though.

runners.comment: None too consistent but low mileage and this return to Class 6 should be in his favour
runners.spotlight: Only had seven runs so still time to do better but his overall record is one of inconsistency; midfield finish at Newmarket seven weeks ago (after an absence) was okay and this is a drop in class; not discounted.

runners.comment: Poor strike-rate but she has been running creditably in turf handicaps this summer
runners.spotlight: Her only win came at Bath, coming from off the pace to land a 5f handicap last August (good to firm); she has finished second in two of her last four starts, the latest back at Bath 33 days ago; vulnerable for win purposes.

runners.comment: Over three years since last of eight wins; 32lb lower now but there are risks attached
runners.spotlight: Prolific course winner but the latest of them was back in April 2022; now rated 32lb lower but he wasn't in much form earlier in the year; could revive after his break; stable also run Galel; betting can guide.

stable_tour.quote: He's owned by a good friend of mine and is a good, rock-solid individual. He won on the turf at Newmarket last year and this term he was successful at Kempton on the all-weather. He's 2lb above his last winning mark and will win again. 23-09-22

runners.comment: Just one win to his name (stable debut) but he ran well last time and Oisin Murphy booked
runners.spotlight: Made a winning stable debut at Southwell in December (6f); not added to that win since but he has run some good races in defeat, including when second at Southwell (5f) 16 days ago; Oisin Murphy booked and he's a leading contender.

runners.comment: Ten-race maiden; some fair turf runs for new stable but needs more to break his duck
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden; three good runs from four attempts for his new stable but he will need to pull out extra if he is to break his duck.

prev_owners.owner: David N Reynolds &  C D Watkins
Qatar Racing Limited & China Horse Club International Ltd

runners.comment: In good form on turf for new stable; four-time AW winner and should remain competitive
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner but he's raced exclusively on turf in four runs for his new stable, winning at Bath in May and a close second at Windsor last month; having his first run at Kempton but no obvious reason why he wouldn't go well here.

quotes.race: Hotel Indigo Bath Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)
quotes.quote: We picked Southbank literally three weeks ago and Stuart Williams [former trainer] had a hand in him being so well. He thrived off the ground - Chris Gordon, trainer.

runners.comment: Inconsistent but ran okay (well backed) when a close 5th at Southwell latest; widest draw
runners.spotlight: Patchy record but he was well backed at Southwell last month and ran a better race, beaten 2l into fifth of the ten runners (behind Top Biller); tongue-tie added today but he has the widest stall to contend with.

runners.comment: Two C&D runs well in excess of anything he's done on turf; big player on stable debut
runners.spotlight: Only 3l behind Time For Sandals over C&D last summer; had just one AW run since, when failing to reward strong market support over C&D in April (finished third off an 11lb higher mark); subsequent turf efforts easy enough to forgive and he makes his stable debut (same owners) off a tempting mark; could be an AW specialist and he's of some interest.

stable_tour.quote: The form of his first two races is strong but he wants better ground than he's had the last twice. He broke slowly from a terrible draw at Chester last time and you can put a line through that. He's rated 71 and should be competitive off that. He could go to Goodwood on Friday. 02-08-24

runners.comment: Two 6f wins this year and ran right up to his best when second at Southwell last month
runners.spotlight: Two 6f wins this year and he ran as well as he ever has when a close second at Southwell last month; 1lb rise shouldn't prevent another big run.

quotes.race: Mira Showers Rain Supreme Handicap
quotes.quote: We certainly weren't expecting that. We thought Top Biller would run well as it was a weak race but Dylan gave him a fantastic ride in grabbing the rail. I really think the return to grass has helped as he was probably getting a bit bored of the all-weather - Shelley Birkett, joint-trainer.

runners.comment: 5f looked inadequate at Lingfield ten days ago; this should be more suitable
runners.spotlight: Unable to land a blow over 5f at Lingfield ten days ago but this trip will be more suitable and he lurks on a dangerous mark.

racecards.verdict: The return to AW looks ideal for EQUALISED (nap) and he can capitalise on his lowly mark on his first run for Harry Eustace.
racecards.betting_forecast: 4/1 Equalised, 5/1 Galel,  Early Release, 6/1 Southbank, 7/1 Top Biller, 12/1 Soar Above,  Sundiata Keita,  Wild Embers, 14/1 Lahina Bay,  Little Miss Magic, 16/1 Vitalline, 25/1 Respectable Jack

racecards.race_name: Go Racing In Yorkshire Future Stars Apprentice Handicap

runners.comment: Back to winning ways at Yarmouth last month; up 4lb but could get a relatively easy lead
runners.spotlight: Had a long absence prior to his reappearance in January; there were some near misses along the way before he finally put his head back in front at Yarmouth 25 days ago, digging deep in a small-field affair (6f, good to firm); 4lb rise asks more of him but he might not have to work too hard to get the lead.

runners.comment: On a losing run but he ran well off this mark twice last month; cards will fall right soon
runners.spotlight: Still to add to his Wolverhampton maiden win (5f) in January 2024; two good placed efforts off this mark last month and the cards will fall right for him one day.

runners.comment: 0-12 for this yard but he's down in the weights and has run some good races, including C&D
runners.spotlight: 0-12 since joining this yard but he has run some solid races, including over C&D and when a close fourth at Wolverhampton last month; one to consider.

runners.comment: All wins over further; may have needed recent return to action but others look more likely
runners.spotlight: All wins over further; two good AW runs when dropped back to 6f in the spring; some of his subsequent lesser efforts came when the yard were quiet and he may have needed his return from a break at Lingfield ten days ago; other still bring more pressing claims.

runners.owner: C Ward, B Kirby & B Valentine
runners.comment: Carlisle winner in July; solid AW runs on last two starts; has course form; not discounted
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways when springing a 28-1 surprise at Carlisle in July (6f, good to soft); comes here on the back of two good AW runs at Southwell; has course form and he's not discounted.

quotes.race: Virgin Bet Ayr Bronze Cup Handicap
quotes.quote: Mattice is only a three-year-old and there could be some good days for him in the future - Tim Easterby, trainer.

prev_owners.owner: Exors Of The Late Mr J D Gordon

runners.comment: C&D winner; scored at Ripon in July and solid effort last time; one to consider
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; rolled back the years with a Ripon success in July (6f, good to soft) when just 4lb lower; another creditable run at Doncaster a fortnight later; each-way shout.

quotes.race: William Hill Silver Trophy Handicap
quotes.quote: I was a bit shocked, but Roundhay Park does turn up every time and tries his heart out. It's lovely getting on such genuine horses. I was doing a rain dance last night as he does like it with a bit of soft in the ground. He's so genuine and kept on finding - Faye McManoman, jockey.

quotes.race: QTS Ayr Bronze Cup Handicap
quotes.quote: Roundhay Park was very relaxed today. He sometimes gets wound up but he came up yesterday and he's been very relaxed. He was given a great ride by Faye as there was no panic and no rush - Nigel Tinkler, trainer.

quotes.race: Racing UK Novice Auction Stakes (Div II)
quotes.quote: Roundhay Park was a bit buzzy so I'll give him a bit of a break now - Nigel Tinkler, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's a lovely horse and has been a great servant for the yard. He was second in the Shergar Cup Sprint Cup years back under Hayley Turner and was a top-class handicapper in his heyday. He finished last of 11 at York on Friday and hasn't shown it in his last two starts but the handicapper has just got him high enough. He's a little bit older now and we could do with a bit of help from the handicapper. 27-07-22

runners.comment: Well suited by the track and twice a runner-up over C&D this year; should be involved
runners.spotlight: Two 5f wins here last October and runner-up twice over C&D this summer; his form has dipped on his last two starts but he's feasibly weighted and he should give it another good go.

runners.comment: Course win in July (5f, good); fair form in defeat since; needs to raise her game a level
runners.spotlight: 5f win here in July off 4lb lower (good ground); creditable efforts in defeat since but she needs to raise her game a level to come out on top.

racecards.verdict: Cargin Bhui looks set to go close but PROFITEER goes well at Pontefract and is tentatively preferred.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Bill Plumb, 4/1 Cargin Bhui, 9/2 Minnesota Lad, 11/2 Profiteer, 8/1 Roaring Ralph,  Mattice,  Roundhay Park, 10/1 Missmimi

racecards.race_name: Download The Raceday Ready App Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Green early but got there late to make winning debut (5f) 23 days ago; penalty; big player
runners.spotlight: £32,000 yearling, 48,000euros 2yo breeze-up; 14-1 and green in the early stages but he came through late to make a winning debut at Ripon (5f, good to firm) three weeks ago; had to be switched out wide that day and the third was a ready winner next time; 6f promises to suit and he's much respected under a penalty, despite the widest stall.

runners.comment: Showed pace before hampered/weakening in 7f maiden 11 days ago; more to come at this trip
runners.spotlight: 82,000gns yearling; 40-1 and he showed pace before being hampered and weakening in a York maiden (7f, good) 11 days ago; ought to be capable of better and this drop in trip could be the right move.

runners.comment: Definite ability but hanging is holding him back; open to improvement if running straight
runners.spotlight: Some promise, despite hanging badly right, when fifth of nine at Musselburgh (7f; 15-2) on debut at the start of last month; unfortunately that trait got the better of him again when last of nine, beaten 17l, at Ayr (6f, also good) eight days later; open to plenty of improvement if that quirk has been ironed out.

runners.comment: 130,000euros breeze-up; yard can strike with a newcomer; needs monitoring in the market
runners.spotlight: By upwardly mobile sire Hello Youmzain; 20,000euros yearling, 130,000euros breeze-up purchase in May; tenth foal; half-brother to four winners including Darinja (French 6f), Djamba (French 1m) and Djannabi (French 1m); dam French 7.5f/1m winner (including AW; RPR 98), half-sister to top miler Darjina; yard can strike with a newcomer and he needs monitoring closely in the market.

runners.comment: Stepped forward 2nd start but it was still modest form; passed over
runners.spotlight: Stepped forward on his second start but it was still modest form when beaten 11l at Thirsk (7f, good to firm) last month; will be of more interest once handicapped.

runners.comment: 100-1 but encouraging debut fifth (5f, AW); mucked about too and 6f looks the go; player
runners.spotlight: £10,000 yearling; 100-1 but it was an encouraging start when fifth of 13 at Newcastle (5f, AW), when shaping like 6f would suit ten days ago; not a lot went right that day either so the potential is there to do better.

runners.comment: Form of his first two starts needs marked improvement
runners.spotlight: Only 4-1 for his debut but finished last at Beverley (soft) in July; bit better when tongue tied and fifth of eight at Ripon (also 5f, good to firm) three weeks ago but that form still needs marked improvement.

runners.comment: 110,000euros breeze-up; by leading first-season sire Starman; heed the market signals
runners.spotlight: 110,000euros breeze-up buy in May; by leading first-season sire Starman; second foal; dam 5f 2yo winner (RPR 93), half-sister to winners Kingsgate Choice (smart 5f/6f) and Rio Deva (5f/6f), out of 5f-6.5f winner; dam went in at the first time of asking; heed the market signals on debut.

runners.comment: Did plenty wrong but beaten only 3l on debut; form's been knocked but she's not ruled out
runners.spotlight: £5,000 yearling; 80-1 and did plenty wrong but beaten only about 3l on her introduction at Windsor (6f, good) in July; that form's taken quite a few knocks but she's not ruled out.

racecards.verdict: Kind Touch is greatly feared but a chance is taken instead on FOZZIE, who ran well for a fair way on his 7f debut.
racecards.betting_forecast: Evs Kind Touch, 5/1 Master Of Shanghai, 6/1 Qila,  Starmade, 8/1 Fozzie, 10/1 Sea Her Excel, 20/1 Kwidsin, 25/1 Rogue Rebellion, 50/1 Pierre Le Picard

racecards.race_name: Ben And Mary Hibbert Memorial Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.owner: J Munroe, M McHale & R Wreglesworth

prev_owners.owner: J Munroe, M McHale & K Wreglesworth
A Pollock, K Wreglesworth & J Munroe
L Folwell, K Wreglesworth & J Munroe

runners.comment: Stayed on to score (11.5f, soft) 17 days ago; unexposed around this trip; respected
runners.spotlight: Took her Flat turf record to 7-29 when staying on to score at Windsor (11.5f, soft; 25-1) 17 days ago; despite her age she's not fully exposed at this sort of trip and a 2lb nudge looks workable; respected.

quotes.race: Virgin Bet Kilkerran Cup Handicap
quotes.quote: That was brilliant. Yorkshire Lady is so hardy. Serena Brotherton rides her out every day and she's come on no end for her last run when she was fourth at York and ran a blinder. Everyone keeps saying she wants soft ground but she just goes better on it when others don't - Jo Mason, jockey.

runners.comment: Last start on turf was disappointing; solid third on AW (1m4f) 12 days ago; not ruled out
runners.spotlight: Bustled up a 2-7 shot in three-runner Windsor handicap (11.5f; good to firm) in June but her last start on turf was disappointing; solid third on AW at Kempton (1m4f) 12 days ago, though, and she's not ruled out.

quotes.race: Turners Of Soham Fillies' Handicap
quotes.quote: They went a good gallop, which helped, and I followed the Godolphin horse through. Mrs Twig stays very well which helped her - winning rider Tyler Heard

quotes.race: Stradivarius First Foals 2024 Fillies' Handicap
quotes.quote: We've come here expecting to win and once Tom (Marquand) dropped Mrs Twig in the result was not in doubt. We came here from the prize money and she's a fast improving filly - William Muir, joint-trainer.

prev_owners.owner: P Inglett, S De Zoete And C Milln

runners.comment: Back from a big break this year; tailed off latest; needs to prove he's retained ability
runners.spotlight: Won last two in 2023; not much promise on his first start for 478 days when last at Southwell in January but his next outing, after a further 195-day break, looked a run to build on when fifth of eight at Haydock in July; tailed off in the Old Borough Cup back there (also 1m6f, good) 12 days ago, though; this is considerably easier but needs to prove he's retained ability.

quotes.race: Sky Bet Sunday Series Middle Distance Handicap
quotes.quote: I tracked the favourite and got a beautiful split and it was straightforward. Mick was keen to drop In The Breeze in and get him to relax and he was dead right - Tom Marquand, rider.

stable_tour.quote: He is a lovely middle-distance horse we have got out of Roger Charlton's yard having won a Newbury handicap off 75 last season. His last run was a good second on heavy ground in October. I think he is a horse we can do a nice bit with. He will be ready in about a month and will start off on the sand before we campaign him back on the turf. Ultimately he will go hurdling in the autumn. 22-03-22

runners.comment: Progressed this year, winning twice before second 25 days ago; may still be improving
runners.spotlight: Has progressed with each run this year, winning twice before another creditable effort when second at Beverley (1m4f, good to firm) 25 days ago; same mark here and she may not have stopped improving.

runners.comment: Steady improvement in h'caps; beaten a head 17 days ago; gradual progression is appealing
runners.spotlight: Steady improvement in handicaps since June and she won a four-runner Ripon handicap (1m4f, good) in July; 4lb higher but was beaten only a head at Windsor (11.5f, heavy) 17 days ago; up another 2lb but her gradual progression is appealing.

runners.comment: Won in July before following up a fortnight later; third latest; cheekpieces a good angle
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough with a pretty convincing success at Salisbury in July before following up off 4lb higher at Leicester a fortnight later; couldn't complete the hat-trick at Beverley (also 1m4f, good to firm) 25 days ago but her dam and best sibling landed all but one of their combined nine wins in headgear and she's respected in first-time cheekpieces.

racecards.verdict: None of the five fillies and mares can be dismissed but SILVER CHAMBER (nap) gets that little bit better every time she runs.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/4 Silver Chamber, 3/1 Yorkshire Lady, 7/2 Percy's Daydream, 11/2 Twilight Moon, 6/1 Mrs Twig, 16/1 In The Breeze

racecards.race_name: Always Trying Racing Syndicate Handicap (Div I)

runners.comment: Veteran who picked up the latest of his eight wins last May; no threat this year though
runners.spotlight: Veteran who picked up the latest of his eight wins when scoring at Ripon (1m2f, good) last May; hasn't threatened in three starts this year, latterly in cheekpieces, and his powers of destruction may finally be on the wane; blinkers back on for the first time in over four years.

quotes.race: Betway Casino Handicap
quotes.quote: Contrast ran well here over a mile last time when he hit a flat spot and the team suggested stepping him up in trip and its worked out well." Fran Berry, rider.

runners.comment: Promise at 1m for new yard but flopped at that trip three weeks ago; first try at 1m2f
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden who is now on his third trainer; promising start for this yard in August when beaten 2l at Ffos Las (good) tackling 1m for the first time; prominent in the market but flopped at that trip at Lingfield (AW) when racing freely three weeks ago; first try at 1m2f.

runners.comment: Similar form in three runs; sibling won twice at about 1m4f; up in trip on h'cap debut
runners.spotlight: Similar form in three qualifying runs (7f to 8.5f) on AW and quick turf; winning sibling scored twice at about 1m4f so may do better up in trip for this handicap debut; interesting to see how he goes in the market for a race that probably won't take much winning.

runners.comment: Placed in 3 h'caps (1m2f) this year; sold for £4,000 in July; market watch on stable debut
runners.spotlight: Placed in three 1m2f handicaps in the first half of the year (good and AW) but it was a moderate effort tried at 1m4f at Catterick in May; left Ollie Pears for £4,000 in July; should be happier back at this trip so watch the market on this stable debut.

runners.comment: Gamely rewarded support (7f, AW) in February; 0-9 on turf; first try at 1m2f
runners.spotlight: Gamely rewarded good support when off the mark at Newcastle (7f, AW) in February; however, he needs to produce more than he's managed in five starts back on turf over the summer, now 0-9 on this surface; first try at 1m2f.

prev_owners.owner: A J McNamee & L C McNamee

runners.comment: Won this in 2024; couple of good efforts this year but very moderate run last time
runners.spotlight: Four wins for Joseph Parr in 2024, including this race under Alice Bond when 9lb higher; 0-5 for new stable this year but he ran well at Yarmouth (good) in July and again when second at Brighton (also 1m2f, good to firm) one month ago; very moderate effort back at that track last time, though.

runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xxii & Da Bin Lo

prev_owners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xxiii & M Hulin

runners.comment: Gone off the boil on last two starts (1m4f); drops back in trip with blinkers reached for
runners.spotlight: Last four starts around 1m4f; promising second at Salisbury (good to firm) despite not getting a clear shot at it in July then made most from three rivals at Yarmouth in August; disappointed at Hamilton (also good) next time and finished last on AW three weeks ago; drops back in trip with blinkers reached for in bid to relight the fire.

runners.comment: Much nearer last than first all five starts; heavy defeat last time; tongue-tie goes on
runners.spotlight: Much nearer last than first in all five starts and suffered a heavy defeat last time; tongue-tie goes on.

runners.comment: Second in 1m classified in July; solid third in 1m2f handicap latest; big contender
runners.spotlight: Looked limited in her first two sprint handicaps but much more like it upped to 1m when second of 12 in a Thirsk classified in July; solid effort back in a handicap when third at Leicester (also good) over this trip just eight days later; off since but can probably do better again; likely player.

runners.comment: 0-12 but her last effort was her best, third of ten in 1m2f classified 24 days ago; player
runners.spotlight: 0-12 but her last effort was her best, when third of ten in a Ripon classified (1m2f, good) 24 days ago; solid recent form a big plus in this race.

racecards.verdict: This doesn't look like it will take too much winning and it may pay to take a chance on handicap debutant KINGS COLLEGE.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 Free Pic, 4/1 Bretton Wood, 9/2 Shabu Shabu, 11/2 Salaria, 13/2 Mrbluesky, 9/1 Mc'ted, 10/1 Kings College, 20/1 Contrast, 25/1 Raysham

racecards.race_name: Always Trying Racing Syndicate Handicap (Div II)

runners.breeder: Mountain View Stud & J C B/Stock

prev_owners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

runners.comment: Won 12-runner h'cap (1m1f) in July; excuses last two starts; very much respected
runners.spotlight: Landed a sixth success when winning a 12-runner handicap at Redcar (1m1f, good to soft) in July; kept at in when fourth at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) under a penalty six days later and excuses both starts since; very much respected.

runners.breeder: J Blackburn, S Winter & H Cram
runners.comment: Bounced back from moderate run when 2nd (1m2f) latest; same mark and he's a player
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far when fifth in first two handicaps and he bounced back from a moderate effort over 1m3f when second of 11 returned to 1m2f at Ayr (good) in July; same mark and he's a player.

runners.breeder: Lynch Bages, Camas Park & Summerhill B/S

prev_owners.owner: Laurent Dassault, Daniel-Yves Treves & Frank A McNulty

runners.comment: No notable form in Britain and best watched on this return from a 549-day break
runners.spotlight: Three wins when trained in France, including AW; modest hurdle form for Jonjo O'Neill in late 2022 and she fared little better in three Flat handicaps for this yard across 2023 and 2024; best watched on this return from a 549-day break unless the market suggests otherwise.

stable_tour.quote: She was formerly trained on the Flat in France and then was hurdling with Jonjo O'Neill, and she's on the comeback trail at the moment. We don't know what went wrong with her - we just thought to stick her out in the field for a year and come back to her. She's been utterly disappointing but I'm sure she'll be okay given a bit of time and runs at Pontefract on Thursday. 17-09-25

runners.comment: Won two of last three; escapes a penalty for latest success; 3lb well in; key player
runners.spotlight: Won two of her last three starts, both times at 1m2f under Jake Dickson, and might be a shade unfortunate not to have got the hat-trick, having stumbled and been short of room for the middle leg at Carlisle; escapes a penalty for Leicester performance (good) last week; 3lb well in; key player.

runners.comment: Bumped into one in July; form dipped after; not ruled out from career low; new trip
runners.spotlight: Good third at Hamilton (good) in July and he bumped into an improving filly when a modest second at Ripon (also about 1m, soft) one week later; form has dipped the last twice but he's not totally dismissed from this career-low mark reunited with Oisin Orr; first try at 1m2f.

runners.owner: Royale Racing & Alfa Site Services Ltd
runners.comment: Seems to like it here; 3rd at this track in July; shapes like 1m2f is worth another go
runners.spotlight: Best 2yo run (trained by Craig Lidster) came at this track (6f, good to soft); not quite hit the same heights for this yard but he's edging down the weights and it was a respectable third back here (1m, good) in July, when shaping like this trip is worth another go; another who can't be ignored.

runners.owner: A Quirke & Mrs G B Walford
runners.comment: Breakthrough win (1m2f) in June; respectable efforts on next two starts; possibilities
runners.spotlight: Progressed in her first four handicaps and followed a narrow second at Wetherby in June with a breakthrough success at Beverley (also 1m2f, good to firm) three weeks later; every bit as good when second over the latter C&D next time and it wasn't a bad effort when fifth at Carlisle (1m1f, good) 17 days ago; possibilities.

runners.comment: Handicapper's giving some help but her form needs a big uplift
runners.spotlight: Beaten upwards of 7l in her four handicaps and it was a moderate effort in a series final at Chepstow 24 days ago; handicapper's giving her help but this is a competitive race for the grade.

runners.breeder: Exors Of The Late Mr G J Freyne
runners.comment: Frequently tailed off; 10lb 'wrong'; first-time blinkers need to give him a huge boost
runners.spotlight: 10lb out of the weights and has been tailed off too many times for comfort this year; first-time blinkers need to give him an elephantine boost.

racecards.verdict: This looks competitive but SWEET KISS has struck up a good partnership with Jake Dickson and escapes a penalty for her latest win.
racecards.betting_forecast: 2/1 Sweet Kiss, 7/2 Berkshire Phantom, 4/1 Lights Go Down, 5/1 Quirke On Parole, 6/1 Space Raider, 9/1 Epicurian Lad, 16/1 Sangara, 33/1 Toute Chic, 150/1 Victoria Legend

racecards.race_name: Nadeen Dawson Memorial Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Half-brother to two winners but makes debut aged five and may be best watched
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; half-brother to winners Sooqaan (7f-8.6f; RPR 78) and Diamond Bit (Hong Kong 6f); dam French 5.5f/1m2f winner; makes debut aged five in a first-time tongue-tie and may be best watched.

runners.comment: Absent since last October but showed promise on his three 2yo starts; watch the betting
runners.spotlight: Absent since last October (gelded in interim) but could be a contender if retaining the promise shown on his three 2yo starts (7f/1m, good to firm/good); interesting to see how he goes in the betting.

runners.comment: Promising fifth on debut at Yarmouth in May and he could be a nice prospect
runners.spotlight: Fared best of the newcomers when fifth of 12 at 12-1 on debut at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) in May; the dam of this 240,000gns yearling was a 1m2f Listed winner for the yard and he could be a nice prospect; upset in stalls and withdrawn at Haydock later in May (8-1 at the time); possible player.

runners.comment: Promising start to career in the spring; gelded since; holds leading form claims
runners.spotlight: Displayed promise in the spring on his first three starts (7f/8.6f, AW); returns from a break having been gelded and if he's anywhere near his best he'll be tough to beat for his top yard.

runners.comment: Half-brother to the stable's Group 2 winner Spirit Dancer; newcomer; one of two for yard
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-brother to five winners notably the stable's Spirit Dancer (1m2f Group 2); one of two for the trainer and he's worth a second look on debut, especially if attracting market support.

runners.comment: 4yo who was well beaten at big odds last winter on her two runs for Fionn McSharry
runners.spotlight: 4yo who was well beaten at big odds last winter on her two runs for Fionn McSharry; a transformation is needed on her stable debut.

runners.breeder: G Hedley & Mike Channon Bloodstock Ltd
runners.comment: Soundly beaten fifth on both starts but her Berkshire trainer is 2-3 with his runners here
runners.spotlight: Made debut last month and she's been a soundly beaten fifth on both starts (1m, AW/soft); however, she's a half-sister to her stable's Group 2 winner Opal Tiara and her Berkshire trainer is 2-3 with his runners here; no surprise if she takes a step forward today.

runners.comment: 1,500gns yearling; 1st foal; dam 10.7f winner (RPR 65); may be one for further down line
runners.spotlight: 1,500gns yearling; first foal; dam 10.7f AW winner (RPR 65), half-sister to two winners; this newcomer may be one for further down the line.

runners.comment: Tailed off at massive odds on both starts (1m/7f)
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at massive odds on both starts (1m/7f).

racecards.verdict: This looks a good opportunity for NATIONAL PARK to get off the mark. He showed promise in the spring on his first three starts.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/6 National Park, 11/4 Influential, 4/1 Bambalam, 8/1 Neptune Street, 33/1 Isle Of Illusion, 66/1 Maith Mar Or, 100/1 Jam Lass, 200/1 Sooziesue

racecards.race_name: Racing TV Handicap

runners.comment: Form figures in staying handicaps at this track this year read 211; respected back here
runners.spotlight: Form figures in staying handicaps at this track this year read 211, the latest success coming by 5l over 2m1f (good) last month; heavy defeat on AW last time but respected back here.

stable_tour.quote: He won a maiden hurdle at Sedgefield and then next time they went far too slow for him. We've given him a break and he's going to come back and have a go on the Flat over two miles as he wants a trip. He started off on the Flat and he's done all right since coming here. 03-04-24

runners.comment: Not so good over jumps of late; disappointed on last two Flat starts in the spring
runners.spotlight: Has been jumping and he won a four-runner Hexham chase in June; not so good in that sphere of late, however, and he disappointed on his last two Flat starts in the spring (including here).

quotes.race: Living North Handicap Chase (Go North Monet's Garden Series Qualifier)
quotes.quote: Glory And Honour is a lovely horse and a joy to ride. He needs a really good gallop as he's such a strong traveller and doesn't find as much as you think he would. Today was an ideal scenario and it played into his hands. It was a nice opportunity to get his head back in front - Jonathan England, jockey.

quotes.race: bet365 Novices' Limited Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: I thought Glory And Honour would appreciate the good ground. He was 3lb better off with the favourite so I couldn't see why he started the price he did - Sam England, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He's arguably the best value-for-money horse we've bought. He cost £13,000 and since we purchased him last August he's never been out of the first three and has won £55,000 in prize-money. He's won twice for us, including the big £40k handicap chase at Doncaster in December. He's still running well and he'll go to Cartmel on June 30 for a big £40,000 chase - I think he'll run a big race. 14-06-24

runners.comment: Regressed over hurdles this year for Kerry Lee and recent Flat form is none too inspiring
runners.spotlight: Regressed over hurdles this year for Kerry Lee and recent Flat form is none too inspiring; beat two home over hurdles 26 days ago.

runners.comment: Having big issues at the start; player if getting away on terms; significant 'if', though
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 4yo who was just 7-2 at Newmarket (2m) last month, when his rider lost his irons leaving the stalls and he was pulled up about 6f out; made favourite in first-time blinkers at Southwell (1m6f, AW) last time but the start proved troublesome again, very slowly away only to finish fourth of eleven; a clean break would put him right in the picture but that's by no means certain.

runners.comment: Good start for this yard when staying-on second (2m, good) in July; contender.
runners.spotlight: Good start for this yard when 22-1 but a staying-on second at Musselburgh (2m, good) in July; contender.

quotes.race: Fitzdares Cartmel Cup Handicap Hurdle
quotes.quote: Young Merlin has won nicely in the end, certainly not from the position I wanted to be in but luckily he got me out of trouble. He came to Amy [Murphy] to freshen him up and she has done a brilliant job with him - Jack Quinlan, rider, speaking to RacingTV.

runners.comment: Form figures this term read 2123, the win (2m, good to firm) coming in June; solid.
runners.spotlight: Course winner (extended 2m5f) who was third in this last year; form figures this term read 2123, the win coming at Newmarket (2m, good to firm) in June; solid.

quotes.race: Boodles Handicap (A Jockey Club Grassroots Stayers Series Qualifier)
quotes.quote: Dereham is a remarkable horse and we wouldn't rule out coming back next year. This is the only race he gets to run in here and the race could not have panned out any better - John Berry, trainer.

runners.comment: Unexposed but was beaten 22l, despite finishing fifth of 12, on h'cap debut; visor goes on
runners.spotlight: Unexposed but was beaten 22l, despite finishing fifth of 12, on her handicap debut at Southwell (1m6f, AW) 24 days ago; first-time visor.

racecards.verdict: Third in this last year, DEREHAM returns in good nick and looks almost certain to run his race again.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/2 Captain Potter, 3/1 Dereham, 4/1 Dalton Park, 9/2 Young Merlin, 6/1 Glory And Honour, 14/1 Louie's Folly, 20/1 Wasthatok

racecards.race_name: Matty Bown Handicap

runners.comment: Won over 1m in June and July; close third 19 days ago; mark demands extra but respected
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m at Chepstow (under Elizabeth Gale) in June and Sandown in July; Racing League event proved too warm next time but was close third at Beverley (8.5f, good to firm) 19 days ago; this mark demands a touch extra but respected.

quotes.race: bettingsites.co.uk Betting Sites Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier)
quotes.quote: It was a stressful ride. I got locked down the rail and I thought a little bit of space would come but it didn't. Great Blasket has been tough today - I haven't even picked my stick up on him. I'm lucky the gaps came up late on and he went for me - Elizabeth Gale, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

quotes.race: Follow Us On X @betrhino Handicap
quotes.quote: We'll check Great Blasket over tomorrow but as long as he comes out of the race okay I can't think why he wouldn't take his chance. He's a very hardy horse. He won first time for us and we probably didn't know how to ride him the next few times on the all-weather. Kieran Shoemark won on him at Southwell, where we said we'd drop him out and try to get there as late as possible, and since then he's won four from five. The change of tactics has definitely worked in his favour. I think stepping up to higher grade races suits him down to the ground, a harder pace and better quality of horses gives him a better target to aim at - Jamie Insole, joint-trainer.

quotes.race: Coast To Curragh Charity Cycle Handicap (Div II)
quotes.quote: Great Blasket did that nicely and was tough. We were a little bit worried that the ground would be a bit sharp for him but Shane said he travelled around nicely. He'll probably step up to a mile the next day - George Murphy, assistant trainer.

runners.comment: Course winner (6f, soft); encouraging stable debut when 3rd (7f, AW) in July; same mark
runners.spotlight: Won 6f handicap here (soft) for William Haggas just under a year ago; didn't kick on for that yard but it was an encouraging stable debut when favourite and third of 12 at Southwell (7f, AW) in July; same mark and shapes like he's worth another go at 1m.

runners.comment: Won this in 2020; won again over C&D in 7-runner handicap in August; same mark; respected
runners.spotlight: Front-running veteran who has rolled up his sleeves for this race four times, including when winning in 2020 and finishing third last year; won again over C&D when landing a seven-runner handicap in August; back on that mark despite two respectable runs since.

quotes.race: Bishopton Equine Handicap
quotes.quote: Garden Oasis was quickly away and I needed that as I knew there was going to be more pace in the race and, obviously drawn one, I could hold the rail if I was quick out so it was ideal, but I did have to go a little bit quicker than I wanted to into the bend. I got a bit of a breather round it but he does stay a bit further so you can stretch them and make sure they don't come past you at any speed. He's as game as they come and loves it around here - David Allan, jockey, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

quotes.race: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 519 Handicap
quotes.quote: Garden Oasis is still full of enthusiasm. You've got to run the speed off them as he's getting older and he's not got the turn of foot he used to have but he digs in really well for you - David Allan, jockey, speaking on Sky Sports Racing.

quotes.race: Visit attheraces.com Handicap
quotes.quote: Garden Oasis absolutely thrives around this place, and the most important thing was just to jump from the gates fast. He was soon on the front end and dug in well - David Allen, rider

quotes.race: Visit attheraces.com Handicap
quotes.quote: I'd love a yard full of horses like Garden Oasis, he's wonderful. He's very uncomplicated, the more you complicate it the worse he runs. He's tough, he likes it here and he goes on pretty much any ground - Tim Easterby, trainer.

quotes.race: Visit attheraces.com Handicap
quotes.quote: Every time Garden Oasis ran last season he came back not right. He was actually in the sales but we decided not to sell him. It took a long time to get him right in the winter but he's been sound and okay recently and ran a good race at Haydock but just didn't get home. David won it in the first furlong - Tim Easterby, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: He ran a bit disappointingly at Ripon, where he just didn't fire for some reason, but he's come out of that well. A mile is his trip, on good ground. 02-05-22

runners.comment: Nothing to shout about for this yard but attracted notable market interest last two runs
runners.spotlight: Useful in Ireland; nothing to shout about in four starts for this yard but was 15-2 from 22-1 on his penultimate start and 6-1 from 14s last time; something has evidently been expected so interesting to see how he goes in the market this time.

quotes.race: Irish EBF Supporting TREO EILE (C & G) Maiden
quotes.quote: His experience stood to him and it was a good performance. I'm not sure what they are like behind Good Heavens but he couldn't have done it any better today. Getting a lead was a good help to him and he quickened up well - Frank Berry, representing owner JP McManus.

stable_tour.quote: He has plenty of experience from the Flat and has schooled well over hurdles. His Flat form is good enough to suggest he can make the grade as a juvenile hurdler. He went very close off a mark of 98 in the Irish Cambridgeshire, only just beaten by one of our four-year-olds. He's a homebred out of an unraced Galileo mare, so it's not a jumping pedigree, but we've had horses by Zoffany who have taken well to hurdling, and I'm looking forward to getting him started. 20-10-22

runners.comment: Back on the scoresheet at Redcar (1m) last month; last over that C&D a fortnight later
runners.spotlight: Returned from six months off with a game success at Wetherby in June; back on the scoresheet when winning at Redcar (1m, good to firm) last month; last of seven over the latter C&D a fortnight later, though; first-time cheekpieces.

quotes.race: Free Tips Daily On attheraces.com Handicap
quotes.quote: Paladin has been disappointing since he came back from quite a long spell off. We thought a lot of him as a youngster but he's dropped a long way in class and nothing seemed to be going right for him so it's great to see him coming back to something like his old form - Mark Johnston, trainer's representative, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.

runners.comment: Made all (1m1f) on stable/seasonal debut in April; not gone on in five subsequent runs
runners.spotlight: Made all at Musselburgh (1m1f) on stable/seasonal debut in April; not gone on from that in five subsequent runs; edging back down the weights.

runners.comment: Front-runner who won in May and June; building back up; well drawn to attack
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won at Hamilton (1m, good) in May and Carlisle (1m1f, soft) in June; not matched that since but has been building back up and was beaten just a neck at Beverley (8.5f, good to firm) last time; well drawn to attack.

runners.comment: Progressive last year; might have needed recent return; possibilities
runners.spotlight: Progressive last year, winning twice, including an amateur riders' race at Ascot just over a year ago; off for 333 days before bidding to repeat that success (1m, soft) but was beaten 22l; not unreasonable to assume he may have needed it despite the 3-1 starting price; possibilities.

quotes.race: Graham Lee Vickers.Bet Leger Legends Classified Stakes
quotes.quote: I thought I'd got it. Terries Royale is a nice horse but still so babyish. He travelled nearly too well into the race, hit the front and was just green. He got lonely, it was a shame nobody came to me earlier. It's frustrating but there's plenty of improvement to come in him - Megan Nicholls, jockey.

quotes.race: LK Bennett Lady Amateur Jockeys Handicap (For Female Amateur Jockeys)
quotes.quote: Terries Royale has taken me into the race, but when he's got there he's been a bit green and wondered what to do. When Becky Smith came on Shaladar he then had something to concentrate on. He was really gutsy. I had to switch but he saw it out well on ground he clearly loves - Serena Brotherton, jockey.

runners.owner: Rise One Racing And W A Bethell
runners.comment: Ran with credit since joining yard; won last time; grinding progression makes appeal
runners.spotlight: Attractive profile since joining this yard, running with credit all seven starts including wins in two of his last three; up 3lb for scoring at Carlisle (1m1f, good) 17 days ago but the grinding progression makes appeal.

stable_tour.quote: He has run seven times this season, with two wins and five places, so he's been remarkably consistent. We got him leased by his owner-breeder. The Rise One Partnership took one half of him and we've got the other half. He'll go to the sales at the end of the season. Regarding ground, he won in bottomless conditions at Carlisle in July, with my stable girl Hannah Dring on board. So he clearly goes on heavy but, saying that, before the race I was saying “Can we just pull him out?” It was good ground when he won at Carlisle last time. He'll have a couple more runs before the end of the season and has been declared to run at Pontefract on Thursday. 17-09-25

runners.comment: Likes it here; last two runs were respectable; player back at this venue
runners.spotlight: Some of his best runs have come here, including when landing a third C&D success (good to firm) in June; solid effort here next time and last two runs were respectable; player back at this venue.

quotes.race: Every Raceday Is Autism Friendly At Pontefract Handicap
quotes.quote: Last time [met trouble at this course] was a disaster and the cheer I got on the way in I think every man and his dog spotted it and they've all had a few quid on! It went more smoothly today - a stronger gallop helped. Mount King  loves fast ground and he loves Pontefract - David Allan, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

quotes.race: Leslie Burton (Fisher) Handicap
quotes.quote: We bought Mount King second hand at the Horses In Training Sale from Joseph O'Brien and he's been a lovely horse for the owners - Tim Easterby, trainer.

runners.comment: Lingfield win in June looks questionable; handicapper still in an unforgiving mood
runners.spotlight: Very patchy profile and he's yet to reproduce the form of his wide-margin Lingfield win (7.5f, soft) in June, when possibly flattered by bagging the stands' side rail; handicapper still in an unforgiving mood.

racecards.verdict: A good handful to consider but SWIFT SALIAN has been shaping like he's about to peak again and is drawn to attack.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/2 Milteye, 5/1 Swift Salian,  Altmore, 7/1 Great Blasket,  Garden Oasis,  Terries Royale, 10/1 Mount King, 12/1 Alessia Fernanda, 14/1 Good Heavens,  Paladin, 20/1 Beltadaay

racecards.race_name: Dan Hague, Rails Bookmaker Nursery Handicap

runners.comment: Has faced toughish tasks since her Sandown win; possibilities on nursery debut
runners.spotlight: Has faced toughish tasks since her Sandown win (5f, good) and it was probably a useful race at Chester last time (6f) as the winner is in the Cheveley Park and the runner-up was third in a sales race at the Curragh last week after enduring wretched luck; possibilities on nursery debut.

runners.owner: Nick Bradley Racing 16 And Jmr Partners
runners.comment: Ran in a Listed race last time and had previously finished well for second at Haydock
runners.spotlight: Beverley maiden winner over 5f; ran in a Listed race last time and had previously finished well for second at Haydock, just ahead of Vlad; the hood worn for last two races is now discarded; has some chance.

quotes.race: EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (For horses In Band D) (EBF Rest' Qual') (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Saucy Jane had a poor draw at Pontefract and she ran respectably but her homework had been showing us she'd improved. I'm delighted for all of my team, especially Harriett [Bryce], who rides her every day and broke her in as well. She looks like a filly on the up and a nice prospect. We'll see how she gets on when she gets home but we could look at the Hilary Needler Trophy back here [May 24] or the Marygate at York next Friday. We can always dream of Ascot. She looks a nice filly and if she continues to progress there's no reason why we can't look at it - Jack Morland, trainer.

stable_tour.quote: She's a nice filly. She ran very well in the Listed Marygate at York behind Secret Hideaway and arguably would have finished closer if she'd had a bit of cover. We're hopefully going to see the Marygate form franked because there are horses who ran in that race running at Royal Ascot this week. Last time out she went to Chantilly for a Listed race but found it all a bit too much in the preliminaries and boiled over a bit. She's had a bit of a holiday now and was back trotting on Monday. There's a Listed race in Deauville over five furlongs that I'm going to target with her and I think she's a stakes-class filly. 18-06-25

runners.comment: All runs fairly positive and he remains capable of better now into nurseries
runners.spotlight: 135,000gns breeze-up buy; third at top tracks before finishing second to a Haggas horse at Ffos Las (6f, good to soft), where he raced too freely after an early bump; remains capable of better now into nurseries.

runners.comment: Has yet to run a bad race and has gone 3-3 in 5f nurseries; up the weights
runners.spotlight: Has yet to run a bad race and has gone 3-3 in 5f nurseries, last time accounting for one with useful form at Beverley (good to firm; also won on good); effectively 7lb higher here as she's up 4lb and they claimed 3lb last time, but promises to stay 6f.

quotes.race: John William Phillips Memorial Nursery Handicap
quotes.quote: Elara May is in the sales race at Redcar, so we'll see. We'd like to go and she'll get in now, and she's proven she's good enough. She's had three quick races and, yes, she's answered every question, but I think she needs a week or two to relax and see if she improves again with a break. She just keeps improving all the time and every time we ask her to do something she just gets better - Charlie McBride, trainer, speaking on Racing TV.

runners.comment: Non-runner
runners.spotlight: Gelded prior to his narrow winning handicap debut at Windsor (6f, good) and wasn't far away off this 3lb higher mark at Haydock (good to firm); unraced on slow ground but solid otherwise; non-runner.

runners.comment: Nowhere in a deep nursery at the Ebor meeting but can do better at this level
runners.spotlight: Posted progressive RPRs in novices before finishing out the back in a deep 6f nursery at the Ebor meeting (40-1); can do better at this level.

runners.comment: One to be interested in should there be money around ahead of this first handicap
runners.spotlight: Only real blip came in a useful maiden and he has been gelded since finishing third at Windsor in May (6f, good to firm; new tongue-tie); one to be interested in should there be money around ahead of this first handicap.

stable_tour.quote: He's had three runs and ran well when third in a 6f maiden at Windsor  last time. He had a little problem, so he couldn't run when he was due to at Bath this month - he pulled a little muscle but he's fine now. I think we'll probably go to the Super Sprint with him. 02-07-25

runners.comment: Contested strong maidens in first two races and was a lot more competitive at Kempton (7f)
runners.spotlight: Contested strong maidens in his first two races and was a lot more competitive at Kempton (7f), having gone from the front; looks quick enough for 6f and should pay his way in handicaps.

racecards.verdict: A trappy nursery. GOUKEN ran at top tracks before finishing second at Ffos Las where he got lit up after an early bump.
racecards.betting_forecast: 5/2 Gouken, 3/1 Elara May, 5/1 Aspect Island, 13/2 Entailed,  Saucy Jane, 7/1 Coyy, 14/1 Ecclefechan

racecards.race_name: This Effective Winning Post Restricted Maiden Stakes (For horses in Bands B, C and D) (GBB Race)

runners.comment: Fifth of ten at Windsor having made up some late ground from off the pace
runners.spotlight: 75,000euros (breeze-up) 2yo; 9-1 at Windsor (1m, soft) and made up some late ground from off the pace to be fifth of the ten runners; can improve.

runners.comment: 55,000gns yearling; eighth foal; half-brother to useful winner Teodolina (1m; RPR 93)
runners.spotlight: 55,000gns yearling; eighth foal; half-brother to winners Teodolina (1m; RPR 93), On The Balcony (1m1f; 82) and Spiritofhayton (Italian 2m/2m2f hurdle); dam unraced half-sister to Group-placed 6f 2yo winner Remember You; respected newcomer from a powerful yard.

runners.comment: 20,000gns breeze-up buy; trainer-owned newcomer who has been gelded; others preferred
runners.spotlight: 20,000gns breeze-up buy; third foal; dam unplaced 6f-1m (RPR 60), half-sister to useful 5f-7.5f winner Oor Jock; trainer-owned newcomer who has been gelded; others preferred; stable also represented by Ballon Rouge.

runners.breeder: J Hanly, A Stroud And T Stewart
runners.comment: 42,000euros yearling; nicely bred and the yard won this 12 months ago (had experience)
runners.spotlight: 42,000euros yearling; seventh foal; half-brother to winners Lose Yourself (Group-placed 7f 2yo; RPR 102), Astral Path (6f 2yo; 83), King Path (French 1m; 81) and Hafez (Qatari 10.5f/1m3f); dam French/US 7f-8.5f winner (107); nicely bred and the yard won this 12 months ago (had experience).

runners.comment: 55,000euros breeze-up purchase; dam unraced sister to US 7.5f 2yo Listed winner Ari Gold
runners.spotlight: 55,000euros breeze-up purchase; second foal; dam unraced sister to US 7.5f 2yo Listed winner Ari Gold, out of US 5.5f winning sister to 7f/1m Grade 3 winner Matrooh and half-sister to 1m Grade 2 winner Rule; best to let the betting guide.

runners.comment: Bit disappointing last time but had looked promising and he has the best form
runners.spotlight: Group 2 Royal Lodge entry; took a backward step last time at Goodwood (respected opposition) but had shaped well in his first two races, notably the 3l fourth on good to soft at Clairefontaine (1m); has to be of strong interest in this company.

stable_tour.quote: He's one to look out for. He's heading to Deauville on Saturday for a mile maiden and should have a nice chance. He caught the eye when third on his debut at Haydock behind Amakhala Warrior and the form of the race is looking good - the winner has gone in again. 30-07-25

runners.comment: Beaten 15l into third at Kempton (1m) and then finished last at Lingfield (7f)
runners.spotlight: Beaten 15l into third at Kempton (1m) and then finished last at Lingfield (7f); one for handicaps.

runners.comment: Beaten about 8l in 1m/7f races at Chelmsford and cheekpieces are soon turned to
runners.spotlight: Beaten about 8l in 1m/7f races at Chelmsford and cheekpieces are soon turned to.

runners.comment: Had a good early position but weakened to be beaten 11l at Lingfield (7f, good)
runners.spotlight: Had a good early position but weakened to be beaten 11l at Lingfield (7f, good); bred to stay well but best watched for now.

runners.comment: Slowly away, inexperienced and ultimately tailed off when 80-1 at Newmarket (1m)
runners.spotlight: Slowly away, inexperienced and ultimately tailed off when 80-1 at Newmarket (1m, good to firm).

runners.comment: Led early but ended up a 12l fourth of the five runners over 1m at Chelmsford
runners.spotlight: Led early but ended up a 12l fourth of the five runners over 1m at Chelmsford; went off 33-1 that day.

racecards.verdict: A Royal Lodge entry looks fanciful but GREEN FALCON is a strong form contender on his penultimate fourth in France.
racecards.betting_forecast: 15/8 Green Falcon, 3/1 New Bay Star, 7/2 Baltic Fleet, 4/1 Starship Trooper, 10/1 Tai Hang Pegasus, 20/1 Femme Fatale, 33/1 Devil's Brigade, 50/1 Loose Connection,  Ballon Rouge, 100/1 Telfy Boy,  Kalika

racecards.race_name: Local Favourite By The Imperial Hotel Nursery Handicap

runners.comment: Clear of the rest when beating all except the favourite on his handicap debut at Leicester
runners.spotlight: Clear of the remainder when beating all except the favourite on his handicap debut at Leicester (7f, good); promises to be well suited by this extra distance; same mark.

runners.owner: Nick Bradley Racing 16 And L Leverett
runners.comment: First-time blinkers triggered improvement when beating four rivals at Salisbury
runners.spotlight: Was looking modest (including nursery debut) but the first-time blinkers triggered improvement when beating four rivals at Salisbury (1m, good to soft); just 4lb higher.

runners.comment: Beaten at least 8l in 7f maidens but she could find her level now into nurseries
runners.spotlight: Beaten at least 8l in a trio of 7f maidens but no surprise should she find her level now into nurseries; market useful.

runners.comment: Quiet at 5f/6f but bred to get this far and has been gelded ahead of nursery debut
runners.spotlight: Couldn't get excited about his qualifying runs (5f/6f), for all that last time was a bit better; however, returns from a break now gelded and handicap newcomers from this yard always merit a second look; plenty of stamina in his pedigree as well.

runners.comment: Never threatened on nursery debut but was only 4-1 and now goes in a visor
runners.spotlight: Only 4-1 for his first nursery at Chelmsford (7f) but never threatened to pose a challenge; now visored.

runners.owner: The Boys Are Back In Town
runners.comment: Hard to summon up too much enthusiasm on what he's shown thus far (at 7f)
runners.spotlight: Hard to summon up too much enthusiasm on what he's shown thus far at 7f, including a defeat in a seller on debut; handicap debut.

racecards.verdict: A chance is taken on the now-gelded handicap debutant WILD THOUGHTS who is now sent over a more suitable trip.
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/4 Kalokalo, 11/4 Gullane Girl, 7/2 Wild Thoughts, 6/1 Cocktail Lounge, 10/1 Whiskey Neat, 12/1 El Carrusel

racecards.race_name: Boodles Handicap

runners.comment: Smart 2yo but absent for 1,116 days and untried on slower than good; risky
runners.spotlight: Impressed in a Windsor maiden on his 2yo debut (6f, good to firm) and finished second in the Group 2 Richmond Stakes one month later; absent since finishing fourth in a Ripon Listed event 1,116 days ago (gelded in the meantime); handicapper has given him a chance (rated 107 after Goodwood) but the ground is an unknown and he has too much to prove.

runners.comment: Yet to shine in 2025; gelding/headgear could help but slower than good is an unknown
