runners.spotlight: Won the Super Sprint at Newbury on his second 2yo run; struggled in Listed/Group 3 events on his first two runs this year, after which he joined James Tate; low-key handicap debut at York in July and now goes in cheekpieces having been gelded; unraced on slower than good.

quotes.race: Weatherbys Super Sprint Stakes (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: The question mark was dropping back to five furlongs after Caburn won over six on his debut. We were always coming here with the prize-money but, in the first two furlongs, I thought this ain't happening today. To do that on his second start is pretty unreal and the Gimcrack would be an obvious next step. This will be the last time he'll run over five furlongs; onwards and upwards - Jack Jones, trainer.

quotes.race: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
quotes.quote: At home Caburn was showing the right things to be a nice horse but, worryingly, most of the 2yos I've ran so far have ran averagely but I thought he was above them so it was nice to see him put it in on the track. This was a half bit of a stepping stone to the Super Sprint and so far the first leg has gone remarkably well - Jack Jones, trainer, speaking to RTV.

runners.comment: Won at Catterick & Doncaster on first two stable starts; flopped on h'cap debut; unexposed
runners.spotlight: Promise in one run for Eve Johnson Houghton one year ago; returned for new yard with a Catterick maiden win (6f, good to soft) and better form when defying a penalty at Doncaster in June (6f, good to firm); flopped at Ayr on his handicap debut in July but at only 4-1 in a big field and capable of bouncing back.

stable_tour.quote: He's talented and his owners will have a lot of fun with him in nice races over six and seven furlongs this year. He probably won despite the course at Catterick and then followed up in good style at Doncaster, where he made the running into a very strong headwind, which isn't easy to do there. There's plenty of improvement in him. 02-07-25

runners.comment: Bids for a 3rd win in the race; on good mark and in form but best on good ground or faster
runners.spotlight: Three of his four wins have come over C&D, including the corresponding race in 2022 and 2024; only 1lb higher than for last year's success and he has run really well at Ripon in two of his last three starts; would be of major interest were it not for the prospect of slow going (just about handles good to soft but all his best form on good or quicker).

quotes.race: Pavers Foundation Catherine Memorial Sprint Handicap (Heritage Handicap)
quotes.quote: I'm thrilled, this has been the plan since First Folio won at Yarmouth very impressively, with Newmarket on the way. The faster ground definitely helped him out today and we made sure we got him out of the stalls. Credit to Dan, he gave him a very cool ride - James Ferguson, trainer.  This horse probably lacked a bit of experience in a big field last time and I wasn't disappointed with the way he went through the race - his finishing position didn't reflect how well he ran - Daniel Muscutt, jockey.

stable_tour.quote: He is a lovely horse and something of a stable stalwart with three wins under his belt. Although he is consistent he is also still progressive and will run at Kempton on Wednesday. I think he can prove he is able to compete in big handicaps at the top level. I would love to have him teed up for a Wokingham or something of that ilk. He has a lot of faith and stiff 6f races suit him perfectly, but he stays 7f and later this year he could get possibly get even further. 29-03-23

runners.comment: Promising 2yo, especially on slow ground; struggled in 2025 but has his conditions today
runners.spotlight: Promising 2yo who was well suited by slow ground; struggled in three runs this year but should get his ground today and a revival could be on the cards.

racecards.verdict: Question marks over all five runners. BELGRAVE (nap) should have his ground today and he could bounce back to his 2yo best.
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/4 Wicket Keeper,  First Folio, 4/1 Belgrave, 9/2 Caburn, 7/1 Al Karrar

racecards.race_name: La Continental Cafe Of Great Yarmouth Handicap

runners.comment: Seven wins included two last season but well below his best in four runs this campaign
runners.spotlight: Seven wins included two last season but he's been well below his best in four runs this campaign and is one to have reservations about at the minute.

stable_tour.quote: A good old boy who's owned by my racing club. We got him ready early for the slow ground but it never came. We shouldn't have run him at Doncaster because it had dried out, but getting that run into him improved him significantly. He wins two races a year and he's still got the appetite to do it. There's been no rain, so we put him on the all-weather this week at Lingfield, where he finished sixth. 30-04-25

runners.breeder: Airlie Stud & Mrs S M Rogers
runners.owner: J Machale, S Tucker & S Jewell
runners.comment: Four AW wins; 0-9 on turf and never been placed; doesn't arrive here in the best of form
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner who is 0-9 on turf and never been placed; doesn't arrive here in the best of form.

runners.comment: Went close at Southwell on penultimate start and was up in grade last time
runners.spotlight: Both wins over 7f at Kempton; has placed form on turf, including over C&D; went close at Southwell on penultimate start and was up in grade last time; each-way claims.

runners.comment: Dual novice winner for previous yard; has stamina to prove on her first run over 1m
runners.spotlight: Dual novice winner for previous yard; has stamina to prove on her first run over 1m but has run fairly well in her two races for Eve Johnson Houghton; hard to dismiss.

runners.comment: Top-class pedigree and came good on his fifth start when blinkered at Windsor (1m, soft)
runners.spotlight: Top-class pedigree and came good on his fifth start when blinkered at Windsor (1m, soft), where he was nicely on top at the finish; up 6lb but could have turned a corner.

runners.breeder: Boyce Bloodstock & Mrs C E Percival
runners.comment: Penalised for 7f win here on Tuesday but every chance that 1m will suit
runners.spotlight: Last three runs were here; struggled over 6f last month but successful over 7f on either side of that, latterly in workmanlike fashion here on Tuesday (good to soft); carries a penalty but gives the strong impression that 1m will suit.

quotes.race: SSP Signs & Graphics Handicap
quotes.quote: Noble Guest wasn't a cheap yearling at 100,000 guineas, but he clipped heels here one day and it knocked his confidence. He's in here again on Thursday but I doubt he'll run - George Margarson, trainer.

runners.comment: Fairly handicapped on the pick of his AW form, though never produced it last time
runners.spotlight: Only his second run on the grass; fairly handicapped on the pick of his AW form, though never produced it last time at Newcastle.

runners.breeder: C R Withers & Mr & Mrs R Reed

prev_owners.owner: N Bromley, S Spampanato, R And E J Reed

runners.comment: Only 3-30 but some competitive runs on turf this year and again on the AW 12 days ago
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate of 3-30; some competitive runs on turf this year and again on the AW 12 days ago; entitled to have some say.

quotes.race: Download The Vickers.Bet App Handicap (Div I)
quotes.quote: The weather could have been better and we were worried about the easy ground, but Dream Pirate handled it well - Nick Bromley, owner.

runners.comment: Tongue-tie he tried when faring less well at Chelmsford is removed; handicap debut
runners.spotlight: Began career with two encouraging runs this summer and the tongue-tie he tried when faring less well at Chelmsford (1m) is now removed; could have a role to play on handicap debut.

runners.comment: Gelded ahead of handicap debut at Southwell (1m) but never threatened to take a hand
runners.spotlight: Gelded ahead of handicap debut at Southwell (1m) but never threatened to take a hand from off the pace; bit to prove now.

runners.comment: Dual course winner and has made all the last twice at Newmarket; career-high mark
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner who has made all in two 1m Newmarket handicaps on good to soft and has to be respected despite a career-high mark (raised 6lb for last time).

racecards.verdict: The new blinkers enabled LEMURIAN to deliver easily his best form yet to win at Windsor and he's bred to be better than this level.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 Lemurian, 5/1 Dashing Donkey, 6/1 Dream Pirate, 7/1 Noble Guest,  Joseph, 10/1 Maid In Chelsea,  Grey Soul, 12/1 Berry Clever, 16/1 Society Lion,  Lordsbridge Grey, 20/1 Kitaro Kich

racecards.race_name: Goffs Two Million Series Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.comment: Respected class-dropper who won't mind getting back on slower ground
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner; close fifth of seven on belated reappearance at Goodwood (1m4f) and contested another better-grade handicap at Haydock 12 days ago (1m6f, good); respected class-dropper who won't mind getting back on slower ground.

stable_tour.quote: He could go straight to the Northumberland Plate on June 28. He's a soft-ground horse who has been completely marooned this spring. He was one of the favourites for the Plate a couple of years and finished fifth behind Calling The Wind. He's fine, he just wants soft ground and there hasn't been any. 09-06-25

runners.comment: Maiden winner; 0-3 in handicaps but has dropped 4lb and in a lower grade here
runners.spotlight: 1m4f maiden winner who has been beaten 4l and 5l in his best two runs in handicaps (at about 1m4f); has dropped 4lb and he's in a lower grade now.

runners.comment: Good mark but a habitual slow starter and the hood has failed to snap that habit
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped but a habitual slow starter and the hood has failed to snap that habit; too risky.

runners.comment: Second in a novice latest; no better than sixth in three previous handicaps (1m2f-1m6f)
runners.spotlight: Visored the last twice; no match for the winner back in a novice last time (1m4f, good to soft) and no better than sixth in three previous handicaps (1m2f-1m6f); hard to suggest that he's well treated.

runners.comment: Hat-trick bid came up shy at Epsom but in a race that wasn't run to suit
runners.spotlight: Stays further than this so did well to go back-to-back in 1m2f handicaps (good to soft/good), and subsequent Epsom defeat (1m4f; first-time hood) came in a race that wasn't run to suit; respected.

quotes.race: OCS Goodliffe Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (Div II)
quotes.quote: King's Castle travelled into it so well for me. I was really happy, I crept my way around and he picked up beautifully. This has been a long time coming. My odds were bigger than I thought they were going to be but it's a bit of a surprise - Taryn Langley, jockey.

runners.comment: Haydock winner; struggled at Ascot but that race was won by a progressive horse
runners.spotlight: Rallied in the new blinkers to get off the mark at Haydock (11.5f, good; 2lb lower) but there were no excuses from the front when fifth of seven at Ascot (1m4f); that race was won by a progressive horse and he could bounce back over a new trip.

racecards.verdict: A trappy six-runner handicap. Class-dropper ADJUVANT might be worth risking as he has competitive form in far stronger company.
racecards.betting_forecast: 11/4 Kuredu King, 7/2 Adjuvant,  King's Castle, 9/2 Burj Zabeel, 5/1 Sword Of Wessex, 10/1 Appier

racecards.race_name: White Swan Fishermans Fortune Handicap

runners.comment: It wasn't a bad effort at Brighton 18 days ago having been off since March
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on the pick of his 2024 form and it wasn't a bad effort at Brighton 18 days ago having been off since March; considered.

runners.breeder: Saleh Al Homaizi & Imad Al Sagar
runners.comment: Most wins and best form has come on the AW; well held back on turf at Newmarket
runners.spotlight: Most wins and best form has come on the AW and he finished well behind Born A Rebel on his return to turf at Newmarket.

stable_tour.quote: He's a bit of a character who tends to hold a bit back for himself. That said, he has done very well this season with three wins and is now 9lb higher than at the start of the year. I hope we can squeeze some more improvement out of him, possibly over a mile, as his only run when stepped up from 7f to that distance was a winning one last month at Brighton. 23-09-22

runners.comment: Back on a good mark so he's on the shortlist after a return to form at Ffos Las
runners.spotlight: Back on a good mark so he's on the shortlist after a return to form at Ffos Las (extended 7f, good), for all that he has something to prove on ground easier than good.

runners.comment: First run for this yard was better than the second but did run in a Class 4 last time
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f handicap last year for Richard Hannon off 7lb higher; first run for this yard was better than the second but it was a Class 4 last time; could go well.

runners.comment: Recent winner but disappointed off this mark here last time; not the easiest to predict
runners.spotlight: Second career win when beating eight rivals at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) but disappointed here next time off this mark; not easy to predict.

runners.comment: Has form here and he's only 3lb higher than for his recent win at Chelmsford
runners.spotlight: 6f winner here in June and got no luck over 7f back here last month before coming good again at Chelmsford (7f); only 3lb higher and should be involved.

quotes.race: Quinnbet Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap
quotes.quote: Rusheen Boy has never shown that much on grass, so I wasn't expecting him to win like that. Last time was too firm, but this was just right. Those two have both been sleepers - they've fallen down the handicap and were bound to pop up eventually - Shelley Birkett, joint-trainer.

runners.comment: Slowly away on stable debut and unexposed at 7f, so not entirely ruled out
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden who is due a 1lb drop after finishing midfield on stable debut at Lingfield (6f); was slowly away and she's unexposed at 7f, so not entirely ruled out.

runners.comment: Nearer last than first in his three handicaps (6f/7f) and returns from a wind operation
runners.spotlight: Nearer last than first in his three handicaps (6f/7f) and now returns from a wind operation; others safer.

runners.comment: Both wins were in late 2022 and he has been looking regressive this year
runners.spotlight: Both wins were in late 2022 and he has been looking regressive this year; wouldn't be the obvious answer after nearly three months off.

runners.comment: Non-runner
runners.spotlight: Was only just touched off at Ffos Las (extended 7f, good) and couldn't have given his true running at Newmarket last month; wouldn't surprise to see him in the thick of it; non-runner.

runners.comment: Makes handicap/stable debut after a lengthy break and probably best watched
runners.spotlight: Best of three AW runs for Jane Chapple-Hyam was the fifth of nine over 7f at Wolverhampton; makes handicap/stable debut after a lengthy break and probably best watched.

runners.comment: Fine from 7f to 1m+; has finished runner-up in three of her last five handicaps
runners.spotlight: Won over 8.6f at Wolverhampton in May and has finished runner-up in three of her five subsequent handicaps (cheekpieces replaced blinkers the last twice); fine at 7f and 1m; one of the better options.

runners.comment: Modest maiden and down the field in all his handicaps at 1m; now in cheekpices
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden and down the field in all his handicaps at 1m; now in cheekpices.

runners.comment: Sat off a strong pace before finishing well for third on handicap debut at Brighton (7f)
runners.spotlight: Sat off a strong pace before finishing well for third at Brighton (7f, good to firm) and that was a respectable start to life in handicaps; may have more to offer.

runners.comment: Quiet at 1m and the same again upped to 1m2f for handicap debut
runners.spotlight: Quiet at 1m and the same again upped to 1m2f for handicap debut; hard to make a form case and he's 3lb out of the weights.

racecards.verdict: The 4yo SEDGEMOOR won off a higher mark when with the Hannon yard and he contested a Class 4 on second run for this yard.
racecards.betting_forecast: 9/2 Rusheen Boy,  Amber Honey, 6/1 Dion Baker,  Sedgemoor, 7/1 Born A Rebel, 8/1 Clipsham La Habana, 9/1 Naval Ensign,  Chloe's Courage, 12/1 Split Elevens, 14/1 Lordsbridge Havana, 25/1 Impulse Buy, 50/1 Hot Talent,  Hot Shot Lawyer

racecards.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden

runners.comment: Soundly beaten in races over 5f and 7f, unlikely to be any more effective over this trip
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in races over 5f at Down Royal and 7f at Leopardstown; unlikely to be any more effective over this trip.

runners.comment: Slowly away and never involved in a 6f maiden at the Curragh in May, best watched for now
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and never involved in a 6f maiden at the Curragh in May; best watched for now.

runners.comment: Weakened at Navan 12 days ago on debut, will need to pick up more experience
runners.spotlight: Weakened at Navan 12 days ago on debut; will need to pick up more experience.

runners.comment: Half-sister to three winners, the stable had a juvenile winner at Cork last week
runners.spotlight: E82,000 F (E78,000 vendor at Tattersalls Ireland Breeze Up); sixth foal; half-sister to three winners; dam unraced sister to 5f 2yo winner Snow Angel; the stable had a juvenile winner at Cork last week.

runners.comment: E30,000 Breeze Up buy, first foal, dam 1m AW winner, stable enjoying a fine season
runners.spotlight: Has been to the sales four times, latest cost E30,000 at Tattersalls Ireland Breeze Up; first foal; dam 1m AW winner, out of  winning half-sister to 1m2f Listed winner Skiffle; stable enjoying a fine season.

runners.comment: Last of 12 over C&D on debut, fared better at 5f at Down Royal, others are much preferred
runners.spotlight: Last of 12 over C&D on debut in race in which You'll Think Of Me was second; fared better at 5f at Down Royal; others are much preferred

runners.owner: Owen Daley & John J Brennan & Coragh Bloodstock Limited
runners.comment: Unplaced dam is closely related to 1m5f Group 3 winner Mr Singh, sire may supply speed
runners.spotlight: 3,000gns yearling, E55,000 Tattersalls Ireland Breeze Up; fifth foal; dam unplaced, closely related to 1m5f Group 3 winner Mr Singh; sire may supply enough speed to make her competitive.

runners.comment: Slowly away and unable to make any impact on debut, can be left out of calculations
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and unable to make any impact on debut at Navan 12 days ago; can be left out of calculations.

runners.comment: Promising debut second at Leopardstown, failed to match that form at Gowran, down in trip
runners.spotlight: Made an encouraging debut when second of six to the Aidan O'Brien-trained Moments Of Joy at Leopardstown in June; failed to match that form at Gowran; shorter trip may suit.

runners.comment: Half-sister to high-class Aidan O'Brien-trained fillies Wichita and Bubbling, noteworthy
runners.spotlight: 11th foal; half-sister to five winners including two high-class Aidan O'Brien-trained fillies Wichita (7f Group 3 at two, 7f Group 2 at three), and Bubbling (Group 2 Rockfel Stakes); dam German Listed winner, half-sister to US 1m2f Grade 1 winner Desert Blanc; smart pedigree; in good hands.

runners.comment: Beaten 5l at Gowran last time, has plenty of experience, overall credentials appear solid
runners.spotlight: Beaten a head first time out at Listowel; was keeping good company (Lady Iman second) when fourth of six in a 6f Group 2 at the Curragh; found 5f inadequate at Goodwood; trouble in running when fourth over 7f at this venue; 5l second at Gowran last time; overall credentials appear solid.

runners.breeder: The Earl Of Halifax & Lope De Vega Synd
runners.owner: John J Brennan & Owen Daley & Coragh Bloodstock Limited
runners.comment: E68,000 Breeze Up buy, dam placed, half-sister to winners notably Love Locket (7f Group 3)
runners.spotlight: E68,000 Tattersalls Ireland Breeze Up; first foal; dam placed, half-sister to winners, notably Love Locket (7f Group 3), out of 1m4f winner from family of Derby winner High Chaparral.

runners.comment: Half-sister to two winners; dam unplaced in France, not a likely contender on debut
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-sister to two winners; dam unplaced 5f-1m2f in France, closely related to US Grade 2 winner Surya (dam of US 1m1f Grade 1 winner Aruna).

runners.owner: J Kirkland & Mrs J M Ryan
runners.comment: Has shown some potential on both starts, others have more substantial claims
runners.spotlight: Made her debut in a 28-runner maiden at the Curragh; her showing was much better than implied by her finishing position in tenth, since she was second in her group of 11; again indicated ability when a staying-on sixth behind a well-fancied stablemate over C&D on her second start.

runners.comment: E300,000 yearling, half-sister to a stakes winner in UAE, in good hands, worth considering
runners.spotlight: E56,000 foal, E300,000 yearling; seventh foal; half-sister to winners Spirit Of Light (Listed-placed UAE), Leading Spirit (UAE dirt Grade 3; 104) and Book Of Life; dam 6f Listed winner by Invincible Spirit.

runners.comment: Beaten only a neck over 5f here in July, will not need to improve much to go one better
runners.spotlight: Beaten only a neck over 5f here in July, will not need to improve much to go one better; winner finished sixth in a valuable sales race at the Currgah on Sunday.

runners.comment: Went very close over C&D on her second start and looks almost certain to be in the mix
runners.spotlight: 500,000gns yearling; first foal; dam 7f Group 3 winner at three for Aidan O'Brien; looked raw when unplaced favourite at the Curragh; was slightly impeded before weakening late; went very close over C&D on her second start and looks almost certain to be in the mix.

racecards.verdict: Runner-up on her debut over 5f here in July, TORNADO KISS may find enough improvement to go one better in a competitive maiden.
racecards.betting_forecast: 3/1 Tornado Kiss, 7/2 You'll Think Of Me, 6/1 Luna Mia,  Little Sure Shot, 13/2 Lumiere Des Etoile, 8/1 Salama Blue Point, 12/1 Jackie Jump Up,  Micaela De Lujan, 14/1 Gimme Energy, 20/1 Lady Duquesa,  Quinta Girl, 25/1 Jungle Bird,  Baahyeh, 33/1 Lil Hot Shot, 50/1 Divine Blue,  Miss Ivor,  Friendly Invoice

racecards.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden

runners.comment: Closely related to an Italian sprint winner, dam 5f winner, yard 0-8 in 2yo races in 2025
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; closely related to an Italian sprint winner, half-brother to a Swedish winner; dam 5f winner, half-sister to useful 6f winner Smokey Lane; yard 0-8 in juvenile races this year.

runners.comment: 66-1, never involved when last of 28 on debut at the Curragh, impossible to fancy
runners.spotlight: 66-1, stable outsider, never involved when last of 28 on debut at the Curragh; Shane Foley takes over now; impossible to fancy.

runners.comment: Dropped away to finish last off 11 in a 7f maiden at Fairyhouse in July, safe to rule out
runners.spotlight: Massive price in a 7f maiden at Fairyhouse in July; dropped away from two furlongs down to finish last off 11; safe to rule out.

runners.comment: Started slowly and never competitive on debut over C&D, considerable improvement needed
runners.spotlight: Started slowly and never competitive on debut over C&D; considerable improvement needed.

runners.comment: Kept good company last time, more likely to be of interest in handicaps in due course
runners.spotlight: Slow to break, never counted on debut in Leopardstown maiden in July; well beaten in a decent maiden at the Curragh last time; may improve; more likely to be of interest in handicaps in due course.

runners.owner: Joseph E Keeling & Aidan Gleeson & M C Grassick
runners.comment: Half-brother to five winners including the stable's dual Listed winner Texas Rock
runners.spotlight: Tenth foal; half-brother to five winners, including the stable's dual Listed winner Texas Rock; dam unraced half-sister to useful 5f/6f 2yo winner Rosabee.

runners.comment: Half-brother to a Group 3 winner, worth considering with established form in short supply
runners.spotlight: £60,000 Goffs UK Breeze Up purchase; ninth foal; half-brother to four winners, including Group 3 scorer Eddie's Boy; dam placed 5f, half-sister to 5f Listed winner Swiss Lake; likely to be well primed for his debut; well worth considering with established form in short supply; third in a Leopardstown barrier trial last month.

runners.comment: Respected but not sure to benefit from the drop in trip following a satisfactory 7f debut
runners.spotlight: 400,000gns yearling; half-brother by No Nay Never to five winners; dam stayed 1m6f, half-sister to 1,000 Guineas winner Miss France; not sure to benefit from the drop in trip following a satisfactory debut over 7f at the Curragh; respected all the same in a race that may not take much winning.

runners.comment: Late progress on debut, 2l behind Savile Row, trainer also runs newcomer Roaring Fire
runners.spotlight: Mehmas colt; 140,000gns breeze-up buy; dam 5f/6f winner with a solid record at stud, best of her progeny is this colt's brother, Caturra, winner of the Flying Childers and Rose Bowl; was noted making late progress on debut at the Curragh (7f), ending up roughly 2l behind ninth-placed Savile Row.

runners.comment: E260,000 yearling, dam showed little is a half-sister to 7f Group 3 winner Iveagh Gardens
runners.spotlight: E260,000 yearling; third foal; dam failed to win from 19 attempts, half-sister to 7f Group 3 winner Iveagh Gardens, out of unraced half-sister to Coventry Stakes winner CD Europe; interesting for in-form stable.

runners.comment: Signs of progress when beaten only 5l in eighth over C&D, more likely as handicap prospect
runners.spotlight: Dropped away to finish last of 15 first time out at Fairyhouse in June, gelded and showed signs of progress when beaten only 5l in eighth over C&D; more likely as handicap prospect.

runners.comment: 8-1 over C&D, closely matched on that evidence with Stolen Treasure who was a 100-1 shot
runners.spotlight: Only 8-1 for debut in the Ballyhane Stakes; slowly away and never involved; same price over C&D on second start; closely matched on that running with Stolen Treasure who was a 100-1 chance.

racecards.verdict: Not much to go on here. The vote goes to the Michael O'Callaghan-trained newcomer ROARING FIRE who has barrier trial experience
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/4 Roaring Fire, 9/4 Savile Row, 6/1 Sparky Sparky, 8/1 Shadow Run, 10/1 Wicklow Gap, 12/1 Boston Max, 14/1 Port Arthur, 20/1 Oppenheimer,  Stolen Treasure, 25/1 Buster's Universal,  Iff In Doubt, 50/1 Dream Legend

racecards.race_name: Goffs Two Million Series Maiden

runners.comment: 50-1 on debut at Down Royal, lost ground at the start and never in contention, last of 12
runners.spotlight: 50-1 on debut at Down Royal; lost ground at the start and never in contention; last of 12.

runners.comment: Improvement likely from a respectable Curragh debut, though drop in trip  may not be ideal
runners.spotlight: Drop in in trip may not be ideal; however, improvement is likely from a respectable Curragh debut (seventh of 17 in his group) in a 28-runner 6f maiden won by his stablemate Mission Central, a subsequent Group 3 winner; blinkered now.

runners.comment: Placed four times including in a Listed race, experience should be an asset, yard in form
runners.spotlight: Promise in maidens and held his own when third of seven in a 5f Curragh Listed contest last month; split two Ger Lyons-trained runners in a 5f maiden at Navan 12 days later; experience should be an asset and the stable is in good form.

runners.comment: Has shown some promise but seems well held by Namiid on Tipperary running, gelded since
runners.spotlight: Has shown some promise but seems well held by Namiid on Tipperary running in July; gelded since.

runners.breeder: Mr J A Knox And Mrs M A Knox
runners.comment: Second in a Curragh event in which the Norfolk Stakes winner took fourth, leading chance
runners.spotlight: £240,000 yearling; first foal; dam 6f winner; started slowly but showed good pace to get into the race when second in a 5f maiden at the Curragh in April; slightly concerning she has not been seen since; winner Stop The Nation is now rated 94 after a Curragh nursery win, fourth-placed Charles Dawrin won his next three races, including the Norfolk Stakes.

runners.comment: Last of nine over C&D first time out, failed to fare any better at Cork five weeks ago
runners.spotlight: Last of nine over C&D first time out; failed to fare any better at Cork five weeks ago.

runners.comment: Has shown some ability but not enough to suggest he can win a maiden at this stage
runners.spotlight: Ran twice in the early part of the season; showed some ability but not enough to suggest he can win a maiden at this stage; tongue-tie added.

runners.comment: Has a speedy pedigree, could go well for a stable not usually associated with 2yo winners
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister to Listed-placed 5f winner La Discoteca and 5f juvenile scorer Tees Aggregates; dam unraced sister to Listed-placed 5f winner Online Alexander; family of Group 1 sprint winner Red Clubs, speedy pedigree; could go well for a stable not usually associated with 2yo winners.

runners.comment: Inexperience may have been a factor when outpaced on debut but drop to 5f may not be ideal
runners.spotlight: Down the field in a Leopardstown barrier trial; not a bad run when sixth of 15 in a 6f maiden at Navan 12 days; got outpaced then before staying to some effect; inexperience may have been a factor; however, drop to 5f is not sure to elicit improvement.

runners.comment: Last of six over 5f at the Curragh on debut, struggled over a longer trip at Roscommon
runners.spotlight: Last of six behind stablemate Miami Destination over 5f at the Curragh on debut; struggled over a longer trip at Roscommon.

runners.comment: Shaped quite well in a barrier trial, little impact at Cork on official debut, cheekpieces
runners.spotlight: E260,000 yearling; half-sister to seven winners; shaped quite well in a barrier trial; little impact at Cork on official debut; cheekpieces on now.

racecards.verdict: The experience gained by NAMIID is seven outings may prove decisive, though she faces a potentially smart filly in Aegina
racecards.betting_forecast: 7/4 Aegina, 9/4 Namiid, 4/1 Controlled, 10/1 Spicetown, 12/1 Schoolyard Days,  Whatastarr, 14/1 Rumba Club, 20/1 Lilbittyprettyone, 25/1 Stonezilian, 50/1 Battleforsupremacy, 66/1 Cheaperbythebottle

racecards.race_name: Aloga Equestrian Nursery Handicap

runners.owner: Kildare Racing Syndicate & Kilmichael Racing Syndicate
runners.comment: Hard to assess on his form in large-field Curragh maidens, may improve with blinkers on
runners.spotlight: Hard to assess on his form in large-field Curragh maidens; may improve with blinkers fitted; in-form stable

runners.comment: C&D fifth on her third start represented a huge improvement, not quite as good at Navan
runners.spotlight: C&D fifth on her third start represented a huge improvement; not quite as good at Navan 12 days ago, leaving with a bit to find with Sup Of Red at these weights.

runners.comment: Last of 12 at the Curragh in her first nursery, blinkers discarded, top apprentice booked
runners.spotlight: First run remains her best, fourth in a six-runner contest at Tipperary in May; can be forgiven her run here when set a very tough task in the Ballyhane Stakes; last of 12 at the Curragh in her first nursery; blinkers discarded; top apprentice booked.

runners.comment: Second at Cork on debut, ordinary form next three starts, Navan fourth offers hope
runners.spotlight: Second at Cork in April on debut. ordinary form next three starts; Navan fourth 12 days ago offers hope; looks well handicapped relative to Hooves Your Daddy on that running.

runners.comment: Never in the hunt from a poor draw at Down Royal on nursery debut, fair run at Bellewstown
runners.spotlight: Fair run when fifth in a Bellewstown maiden; never in the hunt from a poor draw at Down Royal on nursery debut.

runners.owner: Gary Devlin & Kevin Osborne & N W T Dalzell & Tracey Wylie
runners.comment: Has shown a tendency to race too freely in three 5f outings, may slot into handicaps now
runners.spotlight: Has shown a tendency to race too freely in three 5f outings; may slot into handicaps now having obtained a modest mark.

runners.comment: Maiden form appeared to suggest some potential but he has finished ninth in two nurseries
runners.spotlight: Maiden form appeared to suggest some potential; however, he has finished ninth in two nurseries, including a recent Down Royal event in which Believe In Glory was seventh; will need to improve with a visor fitted.

runners.comment: Has competed over range of trips without showing much; booking of Keane the main positive
runners.spotlight: Has competed over a range of trips without showing much; the booking of Colin Keane is the main positive.

runners.owner: John E McEnery & Mrs Pauline Condon
runners.comment: Has finished in front of Cisterna in two nursery clashes, may feature at this modest level
runners.spotlight: Has plenty of experience; placed at a modest level at Hamilton and Beverley; has finished in front of Cisterna in two nursery clashes; may feature at this modest level.

runners.comment: Three-figure odds all three starts, Bellewstown seventh was a step in the right direction
runners.spotlight: Three-figure odds all three runs; Bellewstown seventh was a step in right direction when not far behind The Love Machine; not a lost cause.

runners.comment: Three-figure odds all three starts, very hard to find positives, tongue-tie may help
runners.spotlight: Three-figure odds all three starts, very hard to find positives and the stable seems to have a better chance with The Love Machine; tongue-tie may help.

runners.comment: Weak maiden form, needs transformation in a first-time tongue-tie, a pound out of handicap
runners.spotlight: Weak maiden form; needs transformation in a first-time tongue-tie, a pound out of handicap.

racecards.verdict: Worthwhile form is scarce. SUP OF RED seemed to find her early-season form again when fourth at Navan and may be good enough
racecards.betting_forecast: 4/1 Sup Of Red, 5/1 Believe In Glory, 11/2 Final Melody, 7/1 Hooves Your Daddy, 8/1 Bay Of Supremacy,  Cisterna, 10/1 Monvoe,  Queen Of Newark, 12/1 Gelato,  The Love Machine,  Invincible Rocket, 20/1 Imnotleavinyou

racecards.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Claiming Maiden

runners.comment: Unseated rider on way to start before low-key 40-1 debut; yard 1-15 with 2yos this term
runners.spotlight: Unseated rider on way to the start before his low-key debut at the Curragh last month when sent off 40-1; yard 1-15 with 2yos this term; probably more of a long term prospect.

runners.owner: Westerberg, Mrs J Magnier, M Tabor, D Smith
runners.comment: Promise on debut and was in the first 3 in 2 7f maidens since but form has dipped since
runners.spotlight: Shaped with promise on debut before a third behind Italy at Leopardstown and another good run in defeat when beaten 1l in a 7f Curragh maiden; shown less at 1m since though and now tried without headgear; sets the standard if he can get back to his best.

runners.comment: Galileo Gold filly; 1,800E yearling; half-sister to 3 winners at this trip; hood for debut
runners.spotlight: Galileo Gold filly; cost 1,800Euros as a yearling; half-sister to winners Souter Johnnie (1m AW 2yo; RPR 68), Country Format (Italian 7f/1m AW) and Bridge Too Kabeir (Italian 1m); dam unplaced 5f/6f 2yo (24), half-sister to 6f 2yo Listed winner Shaard; hood for debut probably isn't a good sign.

runners.comment: Minor promise on debut but disappointing in 3 runs since including h'cap debut latest
runners.spotlight: Minor promise when sixth of nine on debut but disappointing in three runs since and didn't show anything when stepped up to 7f on handicap debut earlier this month; up in trip and now tried with cheekpieces; plenty to find.

runners.comment: Low-key efforts in 2 runs so far; cheekpieces on; yard 0-18 with 2yos last 5 years
runners.spotlight: Low-key efforts in two maidens so far, failing to live up to market expectations; may do better now tried with cheekpices, although yard 0-18 with 2yos last five years.

runners.comment: Hasn't beaten many home in three runs at huge odds so far; now tried with cheekpieces
runners.spotlight: Hasn't beaten many home in three runs at huge odds so far; now tried with first-time cheekpieces.

runners.comment: Shown nothing in two runs at triple figure odds so far
runners.spotlight: Shown nothing in two runs at triple figure odds so far.

runners.comment: Struggled in maidens so far; yard get 2yo winners but best watched on back of 4 months off
runners.spotlight: Struggled in trio of maidens so far; yard get 2yo winners but this one probably best watched on back of 122 days off.

runners.comment: Minor promise in maidens; took step forward back at 7f latest; shaped like 1m would suit
runners.spotlight: Only minor promise in maidens but took a step forward when returned to 7f in a handicap last time and shaped like 1m would suit too; will be a major player with a bit more progress.

runners.comment: Waldgeist filly who may need a bit further than this but this won't take much winning
runners.spotlight: Waldgeist filly; closely related to 1m2f Flat (RPR 82) and 2m1f hurdle winner Palamon, half-sister to five winners including Castle Of May (French 1m2f; 94) and Okool (1m2f-1m6f; 94); dam placed 1m2f/1m3f in Germany; made need a bit further than this but not ruled out.

runners.comment: Beaten 11l when 16s for a Down Royal maiden; cheekpieces on; check market
runners.spotlight: Beaten 11l when 16-1 for a Down Royal maiden 13 days ago; cheekpieces on and interesting to see what the market makes of her.

runners.comment: Outpaced over this sort of trip on debut when failing to beat a rival at 50s; big ask here
runners.spotlight: Outpaced over this sort of trip on debut when failing to beat a rival at 50-1; best watched for now.

racecards.verdict: Preference is for DARWIN'S ANGEL who is heading in the right direction after a 4th in a h'cap and she may improve again for further
racecards.betting_forecast: 6/4 Ipanema Beach, 7/2 Darwin's Angel, 8/1 Leblon Sunshine,  Survivor's Code, 10/1 Taiyi, 16/1 Wipeawayyourtears, 20/1 Iamimmaculate,  Moretothis,  Balance Of Trade, 25/1 Badger's Cove,  Zorka, 200/1 Sassy Boom

racecards.race_name: GAIN The Advantage Series Nursery Handicap

runners.owner: Team Valor International LLC & A Oliver
runners.comment: Promising debut before lesser run when fav; better 2nd at Navan latest; tough mark though
runners.spotlight: Promising debut at the Curragh despite odds of 66-1 but disappointed when favourite at Leopardstown second time out; ran with credit from the front in a Navan maiden claimer (1m, yielding) since but doesn't look particularly well treated now handicapping and has stall 15 to negotiate here.

runners.comment: Best run in maidens came when stepped up to 7f latest; new trip/workable mark for hcap bow
runners.spotlight: Best of her three efforts in maidens came when stepped up to 7f here last month; not ruled out over this new trip from a workable handicap mark provided she handles the slower conditions.

runners.comment: No threat in 3 runs at big odds so far and h'capper taken no chances; drawn widest
runners.spotlight: Hasn't been a threat in three starts at big odds so far and handicapper has taken no chances ahead of her handicap debut here; needs improvement for step up in trip; drawn widest.

runners.comment: 0-6 and hasn't improved for handicaps; stiff looking mark at present and has a wide draw
runners.spotlight: 0-6 and hasn't improved for handicaps; stiff looking mark at present and has a wide draw this time.

runners.comment: 80s winner of maiden that worked out; not beaten rival at Pattern level but chance off 68
runners.spotlight: 80-1 winner of a Roscommon maiden that worked out well subsequently; hasn't beaten a rival in two subsequent starts but they have been at Pattern level and must have a chance dropped back in class on handicap debut from a mark of 68.

quotes.race: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
quotes.quote: Leblon Queen was very straightforward. I got a grand tow in, I eased out and got there sooner than ideal, ran around a little bit, but I definitely wasn't running out of petrol because as soon as Dylan [Browne McMonagle, aboard the runner-up] got bang upsides me after the line, she took off and did a lap! I'd say she's a nice filly - Chris Hayes, jockey, speaking to Racing TV.

runners.comment: 3 low-key runs earlier this year; step forward on first run since gelding procedure latest
runners.spotlight: Three low-key runs earlier this year but took a step forward on his first run since a gelding procedure at Killarney last month when staying on for third; big player from the same mark.

runners.comment: Minor promise in Galway maiden on debut but struggled in pair of Gowran runs since
runners.spotlight: Minor promise in a Galway maiden on debut but struggled in a pair of starts at Gowran since; needs a fair chunk of improvement now stepping up in trip handicapping.

runners.comment: Not beaten far in a few of her maiden runs; no progress on h'cap debut over 1m latest
runners.spotlight: Wasn't beaten far in a couple of her maiden runs; didn't progress on handicap debut over this trip last month; a bit more exposed than some.

runners.comment: Modest form in trio of maidens but this trip could suit now h'capping; tongue-tie on
runners.spotlight: Modest form in trio of maidens so far but fair chance this sort of trip will suit now handicapping; wears first-time tongue-tie and worth a market check.

runners.comment: Well-bred Juddmonte filly but has been disappointing in 3 runs so far and hard to fancy
runners.spotlight: Typically well-bred Juddmonte filly but fair to say she's been disappointing so far; finished out the frame when 10-3 for her debut in a Leopardstown maiden over this trip and backwards steps in two subsequent runs at 7f; hard to fancy at present; wears first-time blinkers.

runners.comment: Low-key form in maidens but gave impression this mark will be fine when 4th on h'cap debut
runners.spotlight: Low-key efforts in three runs in maidens but gave the impression this mark will be workable when fourth of eight at the Curragh over 7f; good chance 1m will suit and one for the shortlist.

runners.comment: Eyecatcher over 7f at Down Royal on h'cap debut and 1m likely to suit; up 3lb
runners.spotlight: Struggled in maidens but was an eyecatcher when making up ground from rear at Down Royal 13 days ago when making her handicap debut; shaped like another step up in trip would suit and warrants respect despite 3lb rise.

runners.comment: Poor form in maidens and too keen on Sligo h'ap debut; not sure to want 1m; second-string
runners.spotlight: Poor form in maidens and too keen on handicap debut at Sligo last month; not guaranteed to appreciate 1m and second-string here.

runners.comment: Hasn't shown enough in a trio of maidens at big odds so far; up in trip for handicap debut
runners.spotlight: Hasn't shown enough in a trio of maidens at big odds so far; up in trip for handicap debut.

runners.comment: Hasn't shown anything in four 6f maidens so far; can be slowly away; new trip for h'caps
runners.spotlight: Hasn't shown anything in four 6f maidens so far and can be slowly away; new trip needs to have dramatic effect now handicapping.

runners.comment: Hasn't beaten many home in 3 maidens so far at big odds and he's 5lb wrong for h'cap bow
runners.spotlight: Hasn't beaten many home in three maidens so far at big odds and he's 5lb wrong for handicap debut.

racecards.verdict: After a much improved run on his recent h'cap debut, THE HOLY APOSTLE appeals most from the same mark and there's more to come

racecards.race_name: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden

runners.breeder: D Gibbons, M O'Meara & R Gogan
runners.comment: Never involved when well held in a barrier trial; probably best watched from wide draw
runners.spotlight: Camelot gelding; cost 48,000gns as a yearling; half-brother to five winners including Encrypted (5f-6f including 2yo/AW Listed/US; RPR 110), Gisburn (6f including 2yo; 103), Nigiri (Group-placed 7f/1m; 103) and Impart (5f-6f inc 2yo/AW; 88); dam Listed-placed 1m winner (101); never involved when down the field in a recent barrier trial; probably best watched from wide draw.

runners.owner: Westminster Race Horses & Mr G Schoeningh
runners.comment: Encouraging debut and subsequent 3rd at Galway working out; will handle conditions
runners.spotlight: Encouraging debut at Leopardstown when beaten roughly 6l before hitting the frame in a Galway maiden over this trip (beaten roughly 2l); form has a solid look to it and he's a big player if he takes to the first-time tongue-tie given that we know he'll handle conditions.

runners.breeder: S C E A  Haras De Gratte Panche, M Michel Dardenne, M Samy T
runners.comment: Outran odds of 33-1 when 4th for Sunday's big sales race at the Curragh and met trouble
runners.spotlight: Outran odds of 33-1 when fourth of 16 in Sunday's big sales race at the Curragh; should get similar conditions and it's an effort worth marking up having met a bit of trouble in running; looks capable of going well.

runners.comment: Eyecatching run in recent barrier trial; yard 0-12 with 2yos in last 5 years; tough draw
runners.spotlight: Kodi Bear filly; cost 45,000gns as a yearling; closely related to winners Razeyna (6f/7f; RPR 91) and Game Set (7f including AW; 90); dam placed 7f (71), half-sister to Listed-placed 7f 2yo winner Bayroot, out of unraced half-sister to 1m/1m2f Group 2 winner Kabool; eyecatching run in a barrier trial recently; yard 0-12 with 2yos in last five years and tough draw.

runners.comment: Cotai Glory colt; 60,000gns 2yo; battled on well in recent barrier trial for 2nd
runners.spotlight: Cotai Glory colt; cost 28,000gns as a yearling and 60,000gns as a 2yo breeze-up purchase; brother to 6f 2yo winner Selection (RPR 72), half-brother to three winners including Rollicking (5f-1m including 2yo/Germany; 77) and Masters Apprentice (1m6f AW, 74; 2m hurdle); dam unraced half-sister to 1m Group 2 winner Baqah; battled on well when second in a recent barrier trial; one of the more interesting newcomers but has stall 16 to overcome.

runners.comment: Well held fourth of five when 28-1 at Roscommon on last month's debut; hard to recommend
runners.spotlight: Well held fourth of five when 28-1 at Roscommon on last month's debut; hard to recommend here from wide draw.

runners.comment: No match for first 2 (inc' Johnny Soda) when 3rd in recent barrier trial but not ruled out
runners.spotlight: Sands Of Mali colt; half-brother to winners Rozene (5f including 2yo; RPR 86), Maria Magdalena (11.6f Flat, 68; 2m hurdle) and Behind The Wall (2m hurdle); dam unraced half-sister to three middle-distance Listed winners; no match for first two (including Johnny Soda) when third in barrier trial; yard do well with their juveniles.

runners.comment: Only beaten half a length on debut and worth marking up having been slowly away
runners.spotlight: Was only beaten half a length when runner-up in a 13-runner Tipperary maiden and that's an effort worth marking up having missed the break early; draw could have been kinder but looks like a major player with progress likely.

runners.comment: Showed a bit more on the AW last time out but others have achieved more and vulnerable
runners.spotlight: Showed a bit more on the AW last time out but others have achieved more and vulnerable to improvers.

runners.owner: Ms Denise McCourt & Stephen Sweeney & Maire Bailey

quotes.race: Markel Insurance Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle (Challenger Stayers' Hurdle Series Qualifier)
quotes.quote: Kieren [Buckley] was just saying that he thought his riding had improved since he's come to Ireland and that the old Kieren wouldn't have won on him. He sat going down the hill and gave Sweet Will a beautiful ride, so it was a good ride and good performance a few days on from Fairyhouse - Gavin Cromwell, trainer.

quotes.race: Follow Kilbeggan On Facebook Handicap Chase
quotes.quote: Sweet Will didn't jump terribly well but he dug deep - trainer Gavin Cromwell.

stable_tour.quote: He's a Cheltenham winner this season in a 2m5f conditional jockeys' handicap hurdle in November. After that we gave him a run over fences at Fairyhouse, where he ran a nice race to be second, but he unseated three out at Navan on Sunday. He'll probably run at one of our festivals in the spring. 15-02-23

prev_owners.owner: Exors Of The Late Magalen O Bryant

quotes.race: Naas Racecourse Business Club Limestone Lad Hurdle (Grade 3)
quotes.quote: Zarak The Brave had been doing things nicely at home. He is getting much more settled so I think a longer trip might suit him now. He's in the Champion Hurdle, I don't know if we will go there - Willie Muillins, trainer.

quotes.race: Guinness Galway Hurdle (Grade 3 Handicap)
quotes.quote: That's a class performance. You've got to look at Zarak The Brave now as a Champion Hurdle contender. It's something for a four-year-old to win the race, that says something of how tough the horse is. I thought he was beaten, and it's tough on Noel. I thought Jesse Evans had come to beat him and Paul said to me that he thought he was beaten, too, but when he gave him a smack and changed his whip, the horse got down and galloped again, so that shows how brave he is. Would you look at the Morgiana straight away or maybe a Fishery Lane at Naas first? He's a lightly raced four-year-old so maybe something like that - Willie Mullins, trainer.

quotes.race: Prix Alain du Breil - Course de Haies de Printemps (Hurdle) (Grade 1) (4yo) (Turf)
quotes.quote: Zarak The Brave didn't jump well enough in the first mile. He doesn't have the experience and I would imagine the next time Paul [Townend] will try to stay a bit closer. When he settled him he didn't jump as well - Willie Mullins, trainer.

quotes.race: Starting Point 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
quotes.quote: Zarak The Brave did things right. I was surprised with the turn of foot he showed. He is going to come on for that and he is a nice prospect - Paul Townend, rider.

stable_tour.quote: He was very disappointing at Tipperary, so we decided to give him a bit of a break. He seems fine now and we'll look towards Christmas or the spring for him. 13-11-23

prev_owners.owner: Harry Wigan, Murt Khan & Anita Wigan

stable_tour.quote: He won his first two races over hurdles last season then couldn't build on that as the ground softened. But he was impressive in a handicap at Plumpton on Monday, winning impressively under 12st, and looks as though he is going the right way. 07-11-22
